description:
This package provides a gui program as well as a rviz plugin to publish static transforms.
  Both support the transformation between various Euler angle representations.
  The rviz plugin also allows to configure the transform with an interactive marker.
readme: ROS package tools facilitating definition (static) ROS transforms. extensions rviz: EulerProperty class allowing definition arbitrary sets Euler angles conversion RotationProperty class combining EulerProperty QuaternionProperty provide flexible means entering orientation information. interactive transform publisher rviz::Display plugin allowing define transforms rviz interactive marker. interactively perform frame transformations. cmdline utilities: static_transform_publisher adapted version tf2_ros’s source code, providing fine-grained command-line options define orientations arbitrary set Euler angles. static_transform_publisher_gui interactive version static_transform_publisher allowing modify transform interactively.

This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

description:
A ROS wrapper of the AprilTag 3 visual fiducial detection
    algorithm. Provides full access to the core AprilTag 3 algorithm's
    customizations and makes the tag detection image and detected tags' poses
    available over ROS topics (including tf). The core AprilTag 3 algorithm is
    extended to allow the detection of tag bundles and a bundle calibration
    script is provided (bundle detection is more accurate than single tag
    detection). Continuous (camera image stream) and single image detector nodes
    are available.

description:
This package can be used to generate and manage poses for the direct mode of 3-D
    object search. The direct mode is used as an opening procedure for Active Scene Recognition.
    The poses will be generated so that they cover the current environment map.
    There are different modes to generate these poses. One is based on a grid and a
    second on a recording of the "cropbox record" mode in the asr_state_machine.
authors: Borella Jocelyn, Karrenbauer Oliver, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_direct_search_manager Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_direct_search_manager

description: This package provides a 'perception algorithm'-independent simulation of 6-D object localization for 3D object search by a mobile robot: Based on the poses of the searched objects with respect to the current viewing frustum(s) of the robot, the detectability of the objects is estimated.

description:
This package can generate a grid for the current map. Each grid_point
    defines a position where the robot has to move to. The grid can be used
    later on in the asr_direct_search_manager as a basis to generate poses to
    search the map. At each grid_point the robot will search around him. All
    gird_points together should cover the map.

description:
This package contains nodes which make up the Passive-Scene-Recognition interface to Implicit Shape Model (ISM) trees. The Active-Scene-Recognition interface to ISM trees is located in asr_recognizer_prediction_ism, instead. A short outline of the functionalities provided by the nodes are:
      1. Recording of scenes
      2. Training of an ISM tree (Implicit shape model tree)
      3. Recognition of scenes
      4. Visualization of ISM (tree) data 
	  etc.
authors: Borella Jocelyn, Hanselmann Fabian, Heller Florian, Heizmann Heinrich, Kübler Marcel, Mehlhaus Jonas, Meißner Pascal, Qattan Mohamad, Reckling Reno, Stroh Daniel
readme: asr_ism Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ism

readme: asr_ism_visualizations Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_ism_visualizations

description: This package provides information about the PbD dome's kinematic chain and contains launch-files to publish the chain to a robot_state_publisher. Available launchfiles are: dome with real PTU, dome with simulated PTU, dome with real PTU and Flock of Birds and additional transformation_publisher from guppy-cameras to the kinect cameras mounted above. It also contains the urdf model.
authors: Heizmann Heinrich, Heller Florian, Meißner Pascal, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_kinematic_chain_dome documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_kinematic_chain_dome

description: This package calculates the best approximation for a kinematic chain's parameters using rosenbrock optimization. For calculation, a layout of the kinematic chain must be provided, as well as a set of transformations between two frames of the chain that has been acquired during calibration.
authors: Aumann Florian, Heller Florian, Jäkel Rainer, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_kinematic_chain_optimizer documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_kinematic_chain_optimizer

description: This package contains the ROS-independent library which provides the actual scene-recognition functionality of Implicit Shape Model (ISM) trees. It is referred to both by asr_ism for Passive Scene Recognition and by asr_recognizer_prediction_ism for Active Scene Recognition.

description: This package contains classes and algorithms to predict poses of searched objects by the help of a tree of ISMs. It is organized as a library and contains only a small program to evaluate the performance of different algorithms.
authors: Heizmann Heinrich, Heller Florian, Meißner Pascal, Stöckle Patrick
readme: asr_lib_pose_prediction_ism Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_lib_pose_prediction_ism

description:
This package contains all messages that are particular to our Active Scene Recognition - Framework at 
		Humanoids and Intelligence Systems Lab (HIS), Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT).
	  These messages make up the interfaces between the different collaborating components of this system.
	  They are of critical importance and structured by the ROS communication capabilities.
authors: Kleinert Daniel, Meißner Pascal, Yi Xie
readme: asr_msgs documentation: http://ros.org/wiki/asr_msgs

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com, Felix Marek
readme: asr_navfn documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_navfn

description: This package estimates Next-Best-Views as well as configurations (target positions and orientations) for a robot, where it is most likely to find and recognize searched objects the poses of which have, e.g., been predicted by means of ISM trees.
authors: Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Heller Florian, Meißner Pascal, Schleicher Ralf, Stöckle Patrick, Stroh Daniel, Trautmann Jeremias, Walter Milena, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_next_best_view documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_next_best_view

description: This package is used to store and provide objects and their information which can be used by other packages like object recognizers
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Braun Kai, Heizmann Heinrich, Heller Florian, Kasper Alexander, Marek Felix, Mehlhaus Jonas, Meißner Pascal, Schleicher Ralf, Stöckle Patrick, Walter Milena
readme: asr_object_database Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_object_database

description:
This package contains a system to recognize scenes called the Probabilistic Scene Model (PSM).
    It uses objects and relative poses (relations) between the objects. The realations can be dynamic and each object can be a reference object.
    The system consists of a training subsystem that trains new scenes and a scene inference subsystem that calculates scene probabilities of previously trained scenes.
authors: Braun Kai, Gehrung Joachim, Heizmann Heinrich, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_psm Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_psm

description: Offers visualizations for PSM learner and inference results using RVIZ and gnuplot.
readme: asr_psm_visualizations Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_psm_visualizations

description: This package contains a Ros wrapper for RapidXML (version 1.13)
readme: asr_rapidxml Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_rapidxml

description:
This package includes the algorithmic link between object pose prediction and scene recognition, both based on ISM trees. While it only refers to other packages with respect to the aforementioned
    functionalities, it includes the algorithms realizing Scene Model Sampling. E.g., it provides a (service-based) interface for the scene recognition, including an ASR-optimized visualization of ISM trees.
    The reason for linking scene recognition and object pose prediction within a package instead of message passing, is the size and number of scene recognition results occurring.
authors: Aumann Florian, Heller Florian, Hutmacher Robin, Meißner Pascal, Stöckle Patrick, Stroh Daniel
readme: asr_recognizer_prediction_ism documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_recognizer_prediction_ism

description: This package provides object pose prediction on the basis of the alternative scene model PSM (Probabilistic Scene Model): It generates hypotheses (type and pose) for missing objects in the context of 3D object search. Moreover, it integrates scene recognition and object pose prediction (both on the basis of PSM) into Active Scene Recognition. Its output are asr_next_best_view/AttributedPointCloud.msg messages which can be processed by asr_next_best_view.
readme: asr_recognizer_prediction_psm documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_recognizer_prediction_psm

description: Generates the tree containing selected relations between scene objects from recorded trajectories. Used in PSM.
readme: asr_relation_graph_generator Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_relation_graph_generator

tags: asr resources for active scene recognition
description: This package is the main entry point for Active Scene Recognition (ASR). It contains helpers and resources, including the launch files to start ASR in simulation or on the real mobile robot. Moreover, it includes a customized rviz-configuration file for ASR, databases of recorded scenes and tools which ease the interaction with the ASR system.

readme: asr_resources_for_active_scene_recognition documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_resources_for_active_scene_recognition

description:
This package holds bits and pieces that are related to the acquisition of color and depth-image data streams with robot heads. 
    Currently, it contains a hierarchy of launch scripts to start up simultaneous data acquisition from complex setups of vision sensors as encountered on robot heads 
    (e.g. RGB-D in combination with a color camera or a stereo camera setup).

The asr_ros_uri package delivers functionality equally to resource_finder.
    With one exception: It doesn't load the resource into memory but just delivers the uri path to a given file or a file path for a given uri.

description: This package is used to get poses from Rviz by using the goal tool and to store them in an XML-file

description: This package is used to access the sick lms400 laser range-finder. It contains all necessary functionality to communicate with the sensor via rosmessages.
authors: Aumann Florian, Krehl Yann, Meißner Pascal
readme: asr_sick_lms_400 documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_sick_lms_400

description:
This package implements the logic which connects different functionalities such as (passive) scene recognition, object pose prediction and Next-Best-View estimation
    to Active Scene Recognition. In particular, it is the interface for executing different 3D-object-search procedures one of which Active Scene Recognition is.
    Active Scene Recognition is equivalent to the "object search", which consists of the "direct search" and "indirect search" modes. The search will be executed by
    using different packages of the asr framework.
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Hutmacher Robin, Karrenbauer Oliver, Marek Felix, Meißner Pascal, Trautmann Jeremias, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_state_machine documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_state_machine

description: This package is used for the visualization of the models in the dome/mild environment
authors: Allgeyer Tobias, Braun Kai, Heller Florian, Mehlhaus Jonas, Meißner Pascal, Qattan Mohamad, Wittenbeck Valerij
readme: asr_visualization_server Documentation: http://wiki.ros.org/asr_visualization_server

description:
This package implements the logic of our Online World Model, while the Offline Knowledge Base is distributed across asr_object_database, mild_navigation and asr_visualization_server. It contains four types of information:
      1. All estimations of objects which were localized by object detection (object models)
      2. All views in which objects of given types have been searched due to advice of asr_next_best_view and asr_direct_search_manager
      3. All recognized complete patterns by the recognizer_prediction_ism (scene models)
      4. Some additional information about the Objects: IntermediateObjectWeight, RecognizerName
authors: Aumann Florian, Borella Jocelyn, Hutmacher Robin, Karrenbauer Oliver, Meißner Pascal, Schleicher Ralf, Stöckle Patrick, Trautmann Jeremias

Outputs gnc/ctl/command: force torque commanded control. gnc/ctl/shaper: output GNC command shaper, smooths control. gnc/ctl/traj: trajectory control subsystem commanded follow. gnc/ctl/segment: current segment control subsystem traversing. gnc/ctl/progress: progress executing current segment. GNC Control Wrapper behavior \dotfile ctl_fsm “GNC Control Wrapper finite state machine” width=10cm WAITING: setpoints internal Matlab controller. NOMINAL: Waits start time deferred executions, commands setpoints internal Matlab nominal mode desired trajectory finished. STOPPING: Commands internal Matlab stopping mode. velocity angular velocity fall stop threshould, succeeds. Modes controller internally modes: Idle: control present. Stopping: Position error calculated based delayed current position current position. Velocity acceleration commands Nominal: trajectory position, velocity acceleraion. Stopped: Position/Attitude error Control Velocity acceleration goals Enable/Disable Control case controller tested, enable/disble current controller service gnc/ctl/enable. true enable control false disable requested state active, service return success.

description: This package provides camera utilities.
readme: \page camera Camera library library handles camera calibration parameters transformations, providing functions handle undistorted distorted coordinate frames, helper functions deal reading applying camera transformations. tool named undistort_image undistort optionally crop) set images. undistorted image dimensions, focal length undistorted optical center displayed screen. Usage: export ASTROBEE_RESOURCE_DIR=$SOURCE_PATH/astrobee/resources export ASTROBEE_CONFIG_DIR=$SOURCE_PATH/astrobee/config export ASTROBEE_WORLD=granite export ASTROBEE_ROBOT=p4d undistort_image input_dir/*[0-9].jpg --output_directory output_dir --robot_camera nav_cam Options: –image_list: file list images undistort, line. assumed passed command line. –output_directory: Output directory. undistorted images saved directory inputs. –scale: Undistort images resolution, width multiple scale compared camera model. default –undistorted_crop_win: undistorting, apply crop window dimensions centered undistorted image center. adjusted dimensions optical center printed screen. ‘crop_x crop_y’. ‘loose’ smallest crop window undistorted source pixels optical center centered. –save_bgr: Save undistorted images BGR grayscale. tools expect BGR.) –histogram_equalization: true, histogram equalization. –robot_camera: bot’s cameras Tested nav_cam sci_cam. default nav_cam. –alpha: Add alpha channel areas remapped input transparent. Forces PNG output format. compatible save_bgr. –cubic: expensive cubic interpolation image quality. Notes stitching Astrobee SciCam panoramas, Hugin tools default expect input image frames “rectilinear” camera model. satisfy preprocessing input frames undistort_image. lets lean quality-controlled SciCam calibration adding extra lens parameters complicate Hugin’s huge not-always-stable stitching optimization problem. flags recommended case: –undistorted_crop_win loose: undistorted image general rectangular. argument tells tool tightest crop box saves remapped pixels optical center centered. resulting images smaller uncropped avoid Hugin errors poorly conditioned math overly-wide field view. –alpha: undistorted image rectangular, junk real data borders. default, black, argument adds alpha channel transparent. Luckily, Hugin respect alpha channel input images won’t random black border bits stitched output. –cubic: avoid losing image quality panorama users care Mild quality improvement, takes bit longer.

description: AWS RoboMaker package for accessing the simulation service.
AWS RoboMaker package containing ROS service definitions for service endpoints provided inside of an AWS RoboMaker simulation.
readme: AWS RoboMaker ROS Message Service package ROS service definitions service endpoints AWS RoboMaker simulation. Visit AWS RoboMaker website learn building intelligent robotic applications Amazon Web Services. Install source code workspace. Add .rosinstall file contents workspace folder run rosws update: git: {local-name: src/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs, uri: 'https://github.com/aws-robotics/aws-robomaker-simulation-ros-pkgs.git', version: master} Add dependency package.xml access service types (.srv): <package> <depend>aws_robomaker_simulation_ros_pkgs</depend> </package> Follow Python usage build bundle workspace usual: build ROS rosws update rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src colcon build colcon bundle Usage tag, untag, list tags simulation job ROS command-line ROS application running. AWS RoboMaker documentation. IAM role permissions Replace account# account number. "Version": "Statement": "Action": "robomaker:TagResource", "robomaker:UntagResource", "robomaker:ListTagsForResource" "Resource": "arn:aws:robomaker:*:account#:simulation-job/*" "Effect": AddTags Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.msg import Tag robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import AddTags def add_tags_to_my_sim(): add_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def add_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/add_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestAddTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/add_tags', AddTags) response requestAddTags(tags) response.success: AddTags failed print response.message RemoveTag Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import RemoveTags def remove_tags_from_my_sim(): remove_tags([Tag(key="name", value="my_test")]) def remove_tags(tags): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestRemoveTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/remove_tags', RemoveTags) response requestRemoveTags(tags) response.success: RemoveTags failed print response.message ListTags Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import ListTags def list_tags(): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/list_tags', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestListTags rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/list_tags', ListTags) response requestListTags() response.success: ListTags succeeded print response.tags ListTags failed print response.message CancelSimulation cancel simulation job ROS command-line ROS application running. AWS RoboMaker documentation. IAM role permissions Replace account# account number. "Version": "Statement": "Action": "robomaker:CancelSimulationJob" "Resource": "arn:aws:robomaker:*:account#:simulation-job/*" "Effect": Python import rospy robomaker_simulation_msgs.srv import Cancel def cancel_job(): timeoutInSeconds rospy.wait_for_service('/robomaker/job/cancel', timeout=timeoutInSeconds) requestCancel rospy.ServiceProxy('/robomaker/job/cancel', Cancel) response requestCancel() response.success: Request cancel job failed print response.message License library licensed Apache License.

description:
This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.
readme: BFL: BAYESIAN FILTERING LIBRARY Copyright Klaas Gadeyne dot gmail dot library free software; redistribute and/or modify terms GNU General Public License published Free Software Foundation; version License, option) version. program distributed hope WARRANTY; implied warranty MERCHANTABILITY FITNESS PURPOSE. GNU General Public License details. received copy GNU General Public License program; write Free Software Foundation, Temple Place Suite Boston, USA. library encoporates ideas software libraries: Scene (Andrew Davison). <http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~ajd/Scene/> Bayes++ ACFR). <http://www.acfr.usyd.edu.au/technology/bayesianfilter/Bayes++.htm> CES programming library (Sebastian Thrun). <http://www-2.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs.cmu.edu/user/thrun/public_html/papers/thrun.ces-tr.html> research Bayesian methods compliant motion problems <http://www.mech.kuleuven.be/pma/research/manip/default_en.phtml> important features Released GNU LGPL licence Wrapper matrix RNG libraries, favourite matrix library. wrappers exist matrix/RNG wrapper library LTIlib <http://ltilib.sourceforge.net/doc/homepage/index.shtml>: library algorithms data structures frequently image processing computer vision. NEWMAT <http://www.robertnz.net/nm_intro.htm> Matrix Library boost <http://www.boost.org/> RNG "Bayesian unifying Design". incorporate Bayesian filtering algorithm! filter schemes implemented. Standard EKF, IEKF Non-minimal State <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~tlefebvr/publicaties/BayesStat.ps.gz> Standard Particle filter (arbitrary proposal), BootstrapFilter (Proposal System Model PDF), Auxiliary Particle filter, Extended Kalman Particle Filter. details design ideas, poster library presented Valencia conference Bayesian Statistics, <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~kgadeyne/doctoraat.html> filtering libraries home page <http://www.orocos.org/bfl> Tinne Laet Contributed tutorial website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/getting_started_guide/getting_started_guide.html> discusses construct filter bfl. Wim Meeussen Tinne Laet contributed installation guide website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/installation_guide/installation_guide.html>

description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

    heartbeat times out.

C++ implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.

description:
Provides a toolchain running through the robot calibration process. This
     involves capturing calibration data, estimating parameters, and
     then updating the URDF.

Runs an optimization to estimate the a robot's kinematic parameters. This package is a
    generic rewrite of pr2_calibration_estimation.

This package contains a collection of launch files that can be helpful in configuring
    the calibration stack to run on your robot.

This package defines messages for storing calibration samples
     to be used in full robot calibration procedures. This package
     is still unstable. Expect the messages to change.

This package contains a script to generate calibration launch and configurationfiles for your robot.
    which is based on Michael Ferguson's calibration code

Provide a node that extracts checkerboard corners from ROS images.
    This package is still experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Tools for calculating the intersection of interval messages coming
    in on several topics. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

Provides a node that reports how long a subset of joints has been
     settled. That is, it calculates how long a set of joints has remained
     within a specified threshold. This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

Extracts checkerboard corners from a dense laser snapshot.
     This package is experimental and unstable. Expect its APIs to change.

Listens on a ImageFeatures topic, and waits for the data to settle.
     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

Defines helper functions and routines that greatly help when trying to create a settler
    for a specific sensor channel. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

description: The Clover package
readme: clover ROS package bundle autonomous navigation drone control. Manual installation Install ROS Melodic documentation, create Catkin workspace. Clone repo directory ~/catkin_ws/src/clover: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/CopterExpress/clover.git clover required ROS packages (including mavros opencv) installed rosdep: ~/catkin_ws/ rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Build ROS packages memory constrained platforms key): ~/catkin_ws catkin_make complete mavros install you’ll install geographiclib datasets: curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mavlink/mavros/master/mavros/scripts/install_geographiclib_datasets.sh sudo bash optionally install udev rules provide /dev/px4fmu symlink PX4-based flight controller connected USB. Copy 99-px4fmu.rules /lib/udev/rules.d folder: ~/catkin_ws/src/clover/clover/config sudo 99-px4fmu.rules /lib/udev/rules.d Alternatively change fcu_url property mavros.launch file point flight controller device. Running start connection flight controller, roslaunch clover clover.launch simulation information article. Note package configured connect /dev/px4fmu default previous section). Install udev rules path FCU device mavros.launch.

description: Blockly programming support for Clover
readme: clover_blocks Blockly programming support Clover. user documentation main Clover documentation site. Internal package documentation Frontend frontend files located www subdirectory. frontend application roblib.js library communicating backend node ROS resources. clover_blocks node clover_blocks is blocks programming backend, implementing services topics needed running Blockly-generated Python script. Services ~run (clover_blocks/Run) run Blockly-generated program Python). ~stop (std_srvs/Trigger) terminate running program. ~store (clover_blocks/load) store user program <package_path>/programs default). ~load (clover_blocks/load) load stored programs. Parameters ~programs_dir (string) – directory user programs. Parameters read frontend: ~navigate_tolerance (float) distance tolerance meters, navigate-like blocks (default: ~yaw_tolerance (float) yaw angle tolerance degrees, set_yaw block (default: ~sleep_time (float) duration sleep loop cycles, navigate-like blocks (default: ~confirm_run (bool) enable confirmation run program (default: true). parameters set URL GET-parameters, http://<hostname>/clover_blocks/?navigate_tolerance=0.5&sleep_time=0.1 Topics Published ~running (std_msgs/Bool) program running. ~block (std_msgs/String) current executing block (maximum topic rate limited). ~error (std_msgs/String) – user program errors exceptions. ~prompt (clover_blocks/Prompt) user input request (includes random request string). topic published frontend side: ~prompt/<request_id> (std_msgs/String) – user input response.

description: The cnn_bridge package take freeze graphs and publishes them as ROS messages
readme: Overview package support parsing convolution neural networks (CNN), publishing ROS messages. package supports detection segmentation networks. Input camera topics, OpenCV camera, video. ROS Nodes cnn_publisher ROS node opens freeze graph run images. Publishes detection type cnn_bridge/Detection Detection data. Published boxes, scores, classes. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). segmentation type cnn_bridge/Netmask Segmentation data. Published 2-dimensional array mask values. addition, header image network run statistics, hmi). Parameters source type string required True source images run network. types inputs allowed. path video file OpenCV compatible files work). device number OpenCV device. ROS Image CompressedImage topic. option works ‘usb_cam’ (subscribes ‘usb_cam/image_raw’), ‘cv_cam’ (subscribes ‘cv_camera/image_raw’), ‘ueye’ subscribes ‘ueye_0/image_raw’). logdir type string required True Path hypes file. bellow JSON file. metadata_source type string required True Path metadata file. bellow JSON file. mode type string required True ‘detection’ ‘segmentation’ depending mode. input_tensor type string required True explanatory. output_tensor type string/[string] required True segmentation, explanatory. detection array tensors [boxes,scores,classes] display type Boolean default True display output video_save type Boolean/String default True implemented! save output False disable. True saves ‘/Camera__datetime'. string video title. node saves raw video video mask boxes overlay. running video file, raw saved. cpu type string default False implemented! Sets run network CPU Nvidia GPU present. gpu_percent type Float default Sets percentage Nvidia GPU generally running simultaneous networks. Launch File Examples Start cnn_bridge segmentation mode: roslaunch cnn_bridge segmentation_publisher.launch Start cnn_bridge detection mode: roslaunch cnn_bridge detection_publisher.launch Hypes “frozen_graph_path”: "image_height": "image_width": "resize_image": true, //TODO Add additional fields Metadata JSON segmentation mode: “classes”: [“CLASS_NAME_1”, “CLASS_NAME_2”, “CLASS_NAME_N”] detection mode: “classes”: “color”: (red, green, blue), class outputted network assigned class “id_category”: parent class class dog parent Animal) “category”: parent class class dog parent Animal)

Based reply reply_directly. The 'cob_base_velocity_smoother' reads velocity messages and publishes messages of the same type for "smoothed" velocity to avoid shaking behavior.

This package provides nodes that broadcast tf-frames along various (model-based) Cartesian paths (e.g. Linear, Circular).
    The tf-frames are interpolated using a given velocity profile (e.g. Ramp, Sinoid) and can be used as targets for the cob_frame_tracker/cob_twist_controller.
The cob_collision_velocity_filter package is a package for collision avoidance using teleoperation.

The cob_footprint_observer package adjusts the footprint of the robot based on the setup (e.g. arm and/or tray).
The cob_frame_tracker package contains nodes that publish Twist commands based on the distance to the desired tf frame target.
The cob_hardware_emulation package provides idealized nodes emulating real robot hardware and/or physics simulation.

The cob_mecanum_controller_node provides a lightweight base controller for mecanum drive robots. 
    The out/input for the wheel command/state are wheel velocities in rad/s for the wheels 
    [front left, front right, rear left, rear right]
The cob_model_identifier package provides nodes to analyse the system response behavior of actuators to optimally tune PID controllers to be used with cob_twist_controller framework.

description: The cob_obstacle_distance package calculates distances between both robot links and obstacles to be used for obstacle avoidance within cob_twist_controller framework.
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot omni-directional base platform.
This package provides a trajectory controller which controlls velocities for a chain of joints. This controller can be used e.g. with [[schunk_powercube_chain]].
The cob_omni_drive_controller package provides a ros_controller plugin for the Care-O-bot tricycle base platform.

The main purpose of the cob_twist_controller is to convert target twists into joint velocities. 
  Therefore it makes use of several implemented inverse kinematics approaches at the first order differential level. 
  The inverse differential kinematics solver considers kinematic chain extensions, singularity robustness, 
  redundancy resolution and priority-based methods.
  To avoid hardware destruction there is a limiter interface active as well. 
  Via parameter server users can dynamically configure the solving strategy.
authors: Felix Messmer, Marco Bezzon, Christoph Mark, Francisco Moreno

description: This package contains classes that are able to control the platform of the Care-O-Bot. This means to establish a CAN communication to drive and steering motors of the platform and later send motion commands and receive motor information.

The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".
The cob_driver stack includes packages that provide access to the Care-O-bot hardware through ROS messages, services and actions. E.g. for mobile base, arm, camera sensors, laser scanners, etc...
This packagae implements the special homing procedure that is needed for old cob4/raw bases

The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.

cob_undercarriage_ctrl implements a controller for the omnidirectional base of Care-O-bot 3 on joint level. For a given Pltf-Twist the according wheel steering angles and linear wheel velocities are calculated based on the principle of rigid body motion. Each joint is than controlled individually to achieve the computed position and velocity

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

description: CMake configuration to run coverage
readme: code_coverage ROS package run coverage testing Examples robot_calibration package generate coverage code Travis-CI Codecov.io Usage ROS package: Add code_coverage test depend package.xml Update CMakeLists.txt, testing section add NOTE order test targets coverage macros: if(CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING) find_package(code_coverage REQUIRED) Add compiler flags coverage instrumentation defining targets APPEND_COVERAGE_COMPILER_FLAGS() endif() Add targets (CATKIN_ENABLE_TESTING) Add tests Create target ${PROJECT_NAME}_coverage_report if(ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING) set(COVERAGE_EXCLUDES "*/${PROJECT_NAME}/test*" "*/${PROJECT_NAME}/other_dir_i_dont_care_about*") add_code_coverage( ${PROJECT_NAME}_coverage_report DEPENDENCIES _run_tests_${PROJECT_NAME} endif() endif() Note: variable COVERAGE_EXCLUDES content! build run tests debug build reasonable coverage numbers): CATKIN_MAKE: catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin_make -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report CATKIN_TOOLS: catkin config --cmake-args -DENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING=ON -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug catkin build catkin build PACKAGE_NAME --no-deps --catkin-make-args PACKAGE_NAME_coverage_report output print coverage report located Python rostest Support interface Python-based unit tests require modification coverage information, Python-based nodes run rostest launch files bit additional instrumentation turned <launch> Add argument launch file turn coverage <arg name="coverage" default="false"/> fancy line forces nodes generate coverage <arg name="pythontest_launch_prefix" value="$(eval 'python-coverage run arg('coverage') node generate coverage information <node pkg="example_pkg" name="publisher_node" type="publisher_node.py" node generate coverage <node pkg="example_pkg" name="subscriber_node" type="subscriber_node.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" test generate coverage information include launch-prefix <test time-limit="10" test-name="sample_rostest" pkg="example_pkg" type="sample_rostest.py" launch-prefix="$(arg pythontest_launch_prefix)" </launch> CMakeLists, pass argument: add_rostest(example_rostest.test ARGS coverage:=ENABLE_COVERAGE_TESTING)

description: The CRANE-X7 bringup package
The CRANE-X7 control package
readme: crane_x7_control CRANE-X7のためのパッケージ、 crane_x7_ros のcontrolパッケージです。 crane_x7_controlはU2D2(USB-RS485変換ケーブル)を使用してCRANE-X7のモータ制御を行います。 U2D2はLinuxに接続すると/dev/ttyUSB0として認識されるので、次のコマンドでアクセス権限を変更します。 sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 ケーブルの接続ポート名はデフォルトで/dev/ttyUSB0です。 /dev/ttyUSB1)を使う場合は次のコマンドを実行します。 roslaunch crane_x7_control crane_x7_control.launch port:=/dev/ttyUSB1 crane_x7_controlはデフォルト200Hz周期で制御しています。 制御周期を変更する場合はcrane_x7_control/src/hardware.cppを編集してください。 #define CONTROL_HZ rostopic /crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current subscribed [/crane_x7/crane_x7_control/crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint/current] average rate: min: max: std dev: window: average rate: min: max: std dev: window: 実際の制御周期が200Hzに達せず、100Hz程度に低くなってしまう場合、USB通信ポートのlatency_timerを変更してください。 cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer echo /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer cat /sys/bus/usb-serial/devices/ttyUSB0/latency_timer 参考資料：https://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_wizard2/#usb-latency-setting crane_x7_controlは/crane_x7をルートとするネームスペースにパラメータやトピックを定義します。 各jointについて次のトピックを配信します。 current：電流値[mA] dxl_position：現在角度[360/4096度] temp：温度[度] dynamic_reconfigure crane_x7_controlはdynamic_reconfigureに対応しています。次のコマンドでrqt_reconfigureを起動してアクセスすると各Jointのサーボパラメータを変更することができます。 rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure 各パラメータの詳細についてはROBOTIS公式のXM430およびXM540のサーボマニュアルを参照して下さい。 XM430-W350 XM540-W270 CRANE-X7は位置制御モードと電流制御モードに対応しています。基本設定は位置制御モードになっていますが、複数箇所の設定変更を行うことで電流制御モードへ切り替えることが可能です。 サーボモータのOperating Mode（Address：11）を3：位置制御モードから0：電流制御モードへ設定変更する Dynamixel Wizard crane_x7_controlの設定変更 crane_x7_control/config/crane_x7_control.yamlのハンド部分について次のように変更する gripper_controller: type: "position_controllers/GripperActionController" type: "effort_controllers/GripperActionController" publish_rate: joint: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint gains: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: action_monitor_rate: state_publish_rate: stall_velocity_threshold: goal_tolerance: stall_timeout: dynamixel_port: port_name: "/dev/ttyUSB0" baud_rate: joints: crane_x7_shoulder_fixed_part_pan_joint crane_x7_shoulder_revolute_part_tilt_joint crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_twist_joint crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_rotate_joint crane_x7_lower_arm_fixed_part_joint crane_x7_lower_arm_revolute_part_joint crane_x7_wrist_joint crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint crane_x7_shoulder_fixed_part_pan_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_shoulder_revolute_part_tilt_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_twist_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_upper_arm_revolute_part_rotate_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_lower_arm_fixed_part_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_lower_arm_revolute_part_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_wrist_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: center: home: effort_const: mode: crane_x7_gripper_finger_a_joint: center: home: description:
The ddynamic_reconfigure_python package contains
    a class to instantiate dynamic reconfigure servers on the fly
    registering variables
readme: Dynamic dynamic reconfigure python Register variables dynamic reconfigure server run server fly. .cfg files CMakeLists.txt tinkering! import rospy ddynamic_reconfigure_python.ddynamic_reconfigure import DDynamicReconfigure def dyn_rec_callback(config, level): rospy.loginfo("Received reconf call: str(config)) return config '__main__': rospy.init_node('test_ddynrec') Create D(ynamic)DynamicReconfigure ddynrec DDynamicReconfigure("example_dyn_rec") Add variables description, default min, max, edit_method) ddynrec.add_variable("decimal", "float/double variable", ddynrec.add_variable("integer", "integer variable", ddynrec.add_variable("bool", "bool variable", True) ddynrec.add_variable("string", "string variable", "string dynamic variable") enum_method ddynrec.enum([ ddynrec.const("Small", "int", small constant"), ddynrec.const("Medium", "int", medium constant"), ddynrec.const("Large", "int", large constant"), ddynrec.const("ExtraLarge", "int", extra large constant")], enum ddynrec.add_variable("enumerate", "enumerate variable", edit_method=enum_method) Start server ddynrec.start(dyn_rec_callback) rospy.spin() rosrun ddynamic_reconfigure_python example.py. Run rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure play values. couple examples class scripts folder.

description:

    throughout the driver stacks. It currently contains:

    driver_base: A base class for sensors to provide a consistent state machine
    (retries, error handling, etc.) and interface

    timestamp_tools: Classes to help timestamp hardware events

This package is currently for internal use only. Its API may change
    without warning in the future.  This package is deprecated.

description:
Dynamixel-Workbench is dynamixel solution for ROS.
    This metapackage allows you to easily change the ID, baudrate and operating mode of the Dynamixel.
    Furthermore, it supports various controllers based on operating mode and Dynamixel SDK.
    These controllers are commanded by operators.
This package contains examples of applying the 'dynamixel_workbench_toolbox' library developed on the basis of dynamixel_sdk to various operating modes of Dynamixel.
This package contains nodes that control the Dynamixel by communicating with the server registered in the 'dynamixel_workbench_controllers' package

This package is composed of 'dynamixel_item', 'dynamixel_tool', 'dynamixel_driver' and 'dynamixel_workbench' class.
    The 'dynamixel_item' is saved as control table item and information of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_tool' class loads its by model number of Dynamixels.
    The 'dynamixel_driver' class includes wraped function used in DYNAMIXEL SDK.
    The 'dynamixel_workbench' class make simple to use Dynamixels
readme: run dynamixel_workbench linux ROS Downloads DynamixelSDK git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/DynamixelSDK Build DynamixelSDK http://emanual.robotis.com/docs/en/software/dynamixel/dynamixel_sdk/library_setup/cpp_linux/#build-the-library Downloads Dynamixel-Workbench git clone https://github.com/ROBOTIS-GIT/dynamixel-workbench Build Dynamixel-Workbench Run examples ${YOUR_DOWNLOAD_PATH}/dynamixel_workbench/dynamixel_workbench_toolbox/examples mkdir build build cmake sudo chmod a+rw ${USB_PORT} ./monitor

description:
dynamixel_interface aims to provide a fast, scalable and easily configurable
    interface for controlling the Robotis 'Dynamixel' brand of servo motors. It
    is implemented in c++ and is based on the official robotis dynamixel_sdk.
    The controller is designed to handle many dynamixels at once and can
    synchronously communicate with dynamixels spread across multiple serial ports.
    The controller can run in either position, velocity or torque control modes
    and is easily configured with an external yaml file.
readme: Dynamixel Interface: Fast, Scalable Dynamixel Driver package aims provide scalable easily configurable interface controlling dynamixel’s multiple serial devices. Features: Fast implementation capable controlling dynamixels high rates. Easily configure dynamixels single yaml file Synchronous multiple serial ports threaded dynamixel states/commands exchanged single sensor_msgs/JointState message Supports position, velocity current* control modes. position control mode, supply profile velocities *current control models. series motors supported: PRO PRO+ INSTALLATION Requirements Ubuntu LTS ROS Melodic; Ubuntu LTS ROS Noetic Dependencies dynamixel_sdk Building source mkdir catkin_ws/src catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/csiro-robotics/dynamixel_interface.git rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src catkin build USAGE NOTES detailed instructions, consult tutorials subdirectory package: Tutorial controller Tutorial Multi Port Communications Serial Access serial communications, give user access dialout group sudo usermod dialout $USER Operation dynamixel_interface_controller operate servos: Create controller_config.yaml parameters servo tutorials config/ folder explanation configuration options) Launch dynamixel_interface_controller_node, loading custom config file launch/ folder) operation: controller communicates sensor_msgs::JointState message. Lowering reduce total round-trip time comms bus. recommended set (tested microseconds). values configured PRIOR OPERATION dynamixel wizard robotis. description:
These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types 
     and functions.
Color codes for ansii consoles.

These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters 
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new, 
     templates or exceptions.

This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms. 
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few 
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms, 
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for 
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented 
     with a cross platform set of functions.
Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for 
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.
Metapackage bringing all of ecl together.

Includes basic manipulation related structures as well as a few 
    algorithms currently used at Yujin Robot.

Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just 
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.
Tools and utilities for ecl development.

description: EusLisp meets SLIME
readme: euslime Interactive interpreter EusLisp support completion, on-spot referencing, auto-documetation, definition search, Euslime built top slime runs emacs. quick guide emacs links: GNU Emacs Guided Tour Absolute Beginner’s Guide Emacs Prof. Inaba Suggested Commands Quick Start Install apt install ros-melodic-euslime Configure emacs init file ~/.emacs.el (add-to-list 'load-path "/opt/ros/melodic/share/euslime") (require 'euslime-config) (setq inferior-euslisp-program "roseus") (slime-setup '(slime-fancy slime-banner slime-repl-ansi-color)) Run Open emacs type command: euslime eus10 Compile source eus10 branch: https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/euslime/tree/eus10. command automatically shift apt source euslisp builds. ~/.emacs.el (string-prefix-p "/opt" (getenv "EUSDIR")) (progn (add-to-list 'load-path "/opt/ros/melodic/share/euslime") (setq euslime-compile-path (expand-file-name "~/.euslime_opt/"))) (add-to-list 'load-path "/home/affonso/euslime_ws/install/share/euslime")) (require 'euslime-config) (setq inferior-euslisp-program "roseus") (slime-setup '(slime-fancy slime-banner slime-repl-ansi-color)) Logging setting save logs sessions exit. ~/.emacs.el (defun euslime-save-logs (get-buffer "*slime-events*") (with-current-buffer (get-buffer "*slime-events*") ((logdir (concat (file-name-as-directory euslime-compile-path) "log")) (filename (concat "euslog." (format-time-string (current-time))))) (file-exists-p logdir) (make-directory logdir (write-file (expand-file-name filename logdir)))))) (add-hook 'kill-emacs-hook 'euslime-save-logs) Cheat sheet slime buffer [TAB] completion describe/ apropos apropos package definition [RET] macroexpansion ,quit quit session ,restart-inferior-lisp restart session ,rossetip set ros hostname ,rossetmaster set ros master uri editing buffers TAB completion load expression load-file repl buffer slime buffers quit buffer [RET] select option Build Source Setup Clone code mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src euslime_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/euslime.git Install dependencies rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src Build ~/catkin_ws catkin build Configure emacs init file source directory ~/.emacs.el (add-to-list 'load-path "~/catkin_ws/src/euslime") (require 'euslime-config) (setq inferior-euslisp-program "roseus") (slime-setup '(slime-fancy slime-banner slime-repl-ansi-color)) Run Source package source ~/euslime_ws/install/setup.bash open emacs type command: euslime Run tests Run tests tests/euslime_tests.py RUN TESTS EUSLISP PROGRAM tests/euslime_tests.py eus tests/euslime_tests.py irteusgl tests/euslime_tests.py roseus RUN SINGLE TEST tests/euslime_tests.py eus.test_eval_1 RUN MATCHING TESTS tests/euslime_tests.py eus.test_eval_* tests/euslime_tests.py *.test_eval_1 Works Euslime composed layers software, ultimately connecting emacs interface EusLisp interpreter. description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with
This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description: Fanuc post-processor
readme: Overview repository part ROS-Industrial program. Fanuc Post Processor goal create programs Fanuc robots ROS trajectories. Dependencies wstool Install wstool. rosdep Install, initialize update rosdep. Compiling Create catkin workspace clone project: mkdir catkin_workspace/src catkin_workspace/src git clone https://gitlab.com/InstitutMaupertuis/fanuc_post_processor.git wstool init src src/fanuc_post_processor/fanuc_post_processor.rosinstall Resolve ROS dependencies rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro $ROS_DISTRO Compile catkin_make Testing package includes test showcases usage library: test/program.cpp Troubleshooting upload trouble uploading programs robots, simple tests find what’s wrong: ping robot controller terminal: ping ASCII Upload; favorite web browser address robot check Ascii Upload option Summary Configuration/Status page connect FTP credentials applicable): ftp ftp> open Connected R-30iB FTP server ready. [ArcTool (192.168.100.200:dell): User logged [NORM]. Remote system type UNKNOWN. upload simple program, ROS_TP_PROGRAM.ls /PROG ROS_TP_PROGRAM /ATTR COMMENT "ROS generated"; PROTECT READ_WRITE; DEFAULT_GROUP ROS generated program; DO[5]=ON; WAIT 0.5(sec); DO[5]=OFF; /POS Upload FTP: ftp> put ./ROS_TP_PROGRAM.ls local: ./ROS_TP_PROGRAM.ls remote: ./ROS_TP_PROGRAM.ls PORT command successful. ASCII data connection. ASCII Transfer complete. bytes secs kB/s) upload loops/fails, check Teach Pendant alarm history details error open issue.

description: URDF Macro for adding an adjustable, fath pivot mount used for cameras and other sensors
readme: fath_pivot_mount_description package single URDF macro add manually-adjustable, Fath pivot mount cameras sensors. add mount URDF, add <xacro:include filename="$(find fath_pivot_mount_description)/urdf/lockmount.urdf.xacro" <xacro:fath_pivot_mount prefix="front_camera" parent_link="front_mount" angle="${pi/8}"> <origin xyz="0 rpy="0 </xacro:fath_pivot_mount> <xacro:sensor_d435 parent="front_camera_mount" name="front_realsense"> <origin xyz="0 rpy="0 </xacro:sensor_d435> prefix parameter prepended joints links macro ensure uniqueness. parent_link parameter defines parent fath_pivot_mount. macro automatically creates fixed joint parent_link ${prefix}_base_link. angle parameter pitch angle mount. angle assumed fixed, fath_pivot_mount manually adjusted. mounting surface mount ${prefix}_mount, parent link URDF joints, shown

description: Messages used by Fawkes to interact with ROS.
readme: Fawkes ROS Message Types repository ROS message, service, action specifications access Fawkes features ROS NavGraph navgraph topological graph path planning storing location-specific semantic information related ground map. message types relevant. NavGraph message: navgraph specification- NavGraphEdge sub-message: Edge representation navgraph. NavGraphNode sub-message: Node representation navgraph. NavGraphProperty sub-message: Key-value pair represent properties nodes edges. NavGraphGetPairwiseCosts service Calculates costs pairs nodes graph. Note costly operation. provide full cost specification, planning problems. NavGraphPathCost sub-message: Denote costs path nodes. [NavGraphSearchPath service](srv/NavGraphSearchPath.srv]: Search path nodes navgraph. path returns path sequence nodes total cost. NavGraphGoto action: ROS action move specific node graph. messages processed navgraph-breakout plugin fawkes_navgraph node, reads monitors navgraph YAML file provide API. examples confer rcll_fawkes_sim rosplan_interface_behaviorengine packages. Position exchange object positions universal reference, generic Fawkes ROS object transfer plugin ros-position-3d. Position3D message Position space pose visibility history. history initialization, positive number consecutive positive sightings object, negative number consecutive sensor data frames object visible. history flip negative number positive number visibility toggles. node implement tolerate number frames toggling stability. Behavior Engine Integration interface Lua-based Behavior Engine (project) ROS execute basic actions. SkillStatus message: Describes current status skill execution. ExecSkill action: ROS action execute skill string. PCL Database Fawkes functionality store, retrieve, merge pointclouds MongoDB database. functionality ROS (paper video). StorePointCloud service: Store point cloud database collection. RetrievePointCloud service: Retrieve point cloud database collection time. Result topic point clouds stored (enables trigger external processing pipeline simply invoking restore point cloud). MergePointClouds service: Merge point clouds number timestamps. algorithm paper. result topic. Note requires transform information recorded, typically mongodb_log ROS mongodb-log Fawkes.

description: FCL: the Flexible Collision Library
readme: FCL Flexible Collision Library Linux Windows Coverage FCL library performing types proximity queries pair geometric models composed triangles. Collision detection: detecting models overlap, optionally, triangles overlap. Distance computation: computing minimum distance pair models, distance closest pair points. Tolerance verification: determining models closer tolerance distance. Continuous collision detection: detecting moving models overlap movement, optionally, time contact. Contact information: collision detection continuous collision detection, contact information (including contact normals contact points) returned optionally. FCL features interface Compilable linux win32 makefiles Microsoft Visual projects generated cmake) special topological constraints adjacency information required input models list model’s triangles Supported object shapes: box sphere ellipsoid capsule cone cylinder convex half-space plane mesh octree (optional, octrees represented octomap library http://octomap.github.com) Installation compiling FCL, Eigen libccd collision checking convex objects https://github.com/danfis/libccd) installed. libccd, compile github version zip file webpage, bug fixing included zipped version. optional libraries installed optional capability FCL. octree collision, install octomap library https://octomap.github.io/. CMakeLists.txt generate makefiles Linux Visual studio projects windows. command line, run mkdir build build cmake linux, compile code. windows, generate visual studio project compile code. Interfaces starting proximity computation, set geometry transform objects involving computation. geometry object represented mesh soup, set set mesh triangles vertice indices std::vector<Vector3f> vertices; std::vector<Triangle> triangles; code set vertices triangles BVHModel template class mesh geometry, default OBBRSS template typedef BVHModel<OBBRSSf> Model; std::shared_ptr<Model> geom std::make_shared<Model>(); add mesh data BVHModel structure geom->beginModel(); geom->addSubModel(vertices, triangles); geom->endModel(); transform object includes rotation translation: rotation matrix translation vector Matrix3f Vector3f code setting transform configured Transform3f pose Transform3f::Identity(); pose.linear() pose.translation() geometry transform, combine obtain collision object instance //geom geometry transform object std::shared_ptr<BVHModel<OBBRSSf>> geom Transform3f //Combine CollisionObjectf* obj CollisionObjectf(geom, objects set, perform proximity computation proximity queries FCL follow common pipeline: set query request data structure run query function request input. result returned query result data structure. collision checking, set CollisionRequest data structure, run collision function: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set collision request structure, default setting CollisionRequest request; result returned collision result structure CollisionResult result; perform collision test collide(o1, request, result); setting collision request, user easily choose return contact information slower) return binary collision results faster). distance computation, pipeline objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set distance request structure, default setting DistanceRequest request; result returned collision result structure DistanceResult result; perform distance test distance(o1, request, result); continuous collision, FCL requires goal transform initial transform included collision object data structure). pipeline distance/collision: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* goal transforms Transform3f tf_goal_o1 Transform3f tf_goal_o2 set continuous collision request structure, default setting ContinuousCollisionRequest request; result returned continuous collision result structure ContinuousCollisionResult result; perform continuous collision test continuousCollide(o1, tf_goal_o1, tf_goal_o2, request, result); FCL supports broadphase collision/distance groups objects avoid square complexity. collision, broadphase algorithm return collision pairs. distance, return pair minimum distance. FCL CollisionManager structure manage objects involving collision distance operations. Initialize collision manager group objects. FCL implementations CollisionManager. Generally, DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManager provide performance. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager1 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); Initialize collision manager group objects. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager2 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); add objects collision manager, BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObject() function add object std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects1 for(std::size_t objects1.size(); manager1->registerObject(objects1[i]); choose BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObjects() function add set objects std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects2 manager2->registerObjects(objects2); order collect information broadphase, CollisionManager requires settings: callback collision distance; intermediate data store information generated broadphase computation. convenience, FCL default callbacks satisfy call data satisfy collision distance queries. collision DefaultCollisionCallback DefaultCollisionData distance DefaultDistanceCallback DefaultDistanceData. default collision/distance data structs simply containers include request distance structures query type mentioned DefaultCollisionData collision_data; DefaultDistanceData distance_data; Setup managers, related initializing broadphase acceleration structure objects input manager1->setup(); manager2->setup(); Examples queries Collision query object groups collision numbers manager2->collide(manager1, &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); int n_contact_num collision_data.result.numContacts(); Distance query object groups minimum distance manager2->distance(manager1, &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); double min_distance distance_data.result.min_distance; collision query group manager1->collide(&collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); distance query group manager1->distance(&distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); Collision query object group entire group manager2->collide(objects1[0], &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); Distance query object group entire group manager2->distance(objects1[0], &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); examples, refer test folder: test_fcl_collision.cpp: provide examples collision test test_fcl_distance.cpp: provide examples distance test test_fcl_broadphase.cpp: provide examples broadphase collision/distance test test_fcl_frontlist.cpp: provide examples frontlist collision acceleration test_fcl_octomap.cpp: provide examples collision/distance computation octomap data data types.

description:
The public fetch_drivers package is a binary only release.

    fetch_drivers contains both the drivers and firmware for the fetch and freight research robots.
    There should be no reason to use these drivers unless you're running on a fetch or a freight research robot.
    This package, is a cmake/make only package which installs the binaries for the drivers and firmware.
maintainers: Carl Saldanha, Eric Relson, Sarah Elliott, Fetch Robotics Open Source Team
readme: Fetch Binary Drivers public binary version drivers firmware Fetch Research Platforms. https://fetchrobotics.com/robotics-platforms/ Fetch Research Platforms. Commonly Fetch Freight. Fetch arm. drivers firmware package https://docs.fetchrobotics.com/ Fetch Drivers catkin package folder called fetch_drivers, fetch_binary_drivers. we’ve previously releasing packages depend fetch_drivers. changing distribute drivers. package needed you’re Fetch Research Platforms. goal support Fetch Research Platform customers improved, automated build release process. enable updates faster. discussed ROSCon talk “Hermetic Robot Deployment Multi-Stage Dockers” @levavakian @bluryi internal build/test/deployment Docker: Video, Slides. public repository, designed consume output private fetch_drivers repository enable Fetch Research Platform users access drivers/firmware faster official ros packages. create output private package, special build job runs docker container ensure don’t accidentally pull private dependencies, doesn’t output additional commercial robot drivers. Previously, built drivers private buildbot, hosted packages site. manually synced mirror ros packages. allowed ensure tested versions dependancies mirror. process automated process testing ROS Melodic, setting hosted stable mirror, documenting upgrade process ensure Fetch Research Platform customers smooth transition. announce customers officially ready supporting ROS Melodic. https://docs.fetchrobotics.com information.

description: A meta-package to aggregate all the FlexBE packages
flexbe_core provides the core components for the FlexBE behavior engine.
flexbe_input enables to send data to onboard behavior when required.
flexbe_mirror implements functionality to remotely mirror an executed behavior.
flexbe_msgs provides the messages used by FlexBE.
flexbe_onboard implements the robot-side of the behavior engine from where all behaviors are started.

flexbe_states provides a collection of predefined states.
     Feel free to add new states.
flexbe_testing provides a framework for unit testing states.
flexbe_widget implements some smaller scripts for the behavior engine.
flexbe_app provides a user interface (editor + runtime control) for the FlexBE behavior engine.

description: Wraps FMUs for co-simulation
readme: General information repository, including legal information, build instructions issues/limitations, README.md repository root. fmi_adapter package fmi_adapter small ROS package wrapping functional mockup units (FMUs) co-simulation physical models ROS nodes. FMUs defined FMI standard. package supports co-simulation FMUs FMI standard FMUs created variety modeling simulation tools. Examples Dymola, MATLAB/Simulink, OpenModelica, SimulationX, Wolfram System Modeler. Technically, co-simulation FMU zip file suffix .fmu) physical model solver shared library XML file describing inputs, outputs parameters model details solver configuration. addition, zip file source code model solver programming language. fmi_adapter node fmi_adapter ROS node (fmi_adapter_node.cpp), takes FMU creates subscribers publishers input output variables FMU, runs FMU’s solver user-definable update period ROS clock. approach illustrated diagram. fmi_adapter_node searches counterparts FMU parameter variable ROS parameter server initializes FMU correspondingly. purpose, package provide launch file argument fmu_path. Simply call roslaunch fmi_adapter fmi_adapter_node.launch fmu_path:=[PathToTheFMUFile] README.md fmi_adapter_examples package step-by-step description fmi_adapter node damped pendulum model FMU. fmi_adapter library fmi_adapter library convenience functions based common ROS types load FMU runtime, retrieve input, output, parameter names, set timestamped input values, run FMU’s numeric solver, query resulting output. functions class FMIAdapter. Instances class integrated application-specific ROS nodes libraries illustrated architecture diagram. parsing XML description FMU running FMU’s solver, fmi_adapter library FMI Library. library downloaded, compiled linked CMakeLists.txt package cmake’s externalproject_add command. Running FMU ROS node library give code snippets load run FMU file application-specific ROS node library. Step Include FMIAdapter.h fmi_adapter package code. #include "fmi_adapter/FMIAdapter.h" Step Instantiate adapter class path FMU file desired simulation step size. step-size argument omitted, default step size FMU file ros::Duration stepSize(0.001); fmi_adapter::FMIAdapter adapter(fmuPath, stepSize); Step Create subscribers timers set FMU’s input values. ros::Subscriber subscriber nHandle.subscribe<std_msgs::Float64>("angle_x", [&adapter](const std_msgs::Float64::ConstPtr& msg) adapter.setInputValue("angleX", ros::Time::now(), msg->data); angle_x topic angleX input variable FMU. adapter.getInputVariableNames() list input variables. Step Create timer subscriber triggers simulation FMU adapter.doStepsUntil(..). ros::Timer timer nHandle.createTimer(ros::Duration(0.01), [&](const ros::TimerEvent& event) adapter.doStepsUntil(event.current_expected); double adapter.getOutputValue("angleY"); publish topic adapter.getOutputVariableNames() list output variables. Step Set parameters initial values FMU: adapter.setInitialValue("dampingParam", adapter.setInitialValue("angleX", function adapter.initializeFromROSParameters(nodeHandle) initialize parameters ROS parameters. note characters FMU parameter names supported ROS replaced FMIAdapter::rosifyName(name). Step starting spin thread, exit FMU’s initialization mode set ROS time refers FMU’s internal timepoint adapter.exitInitializationMode(ros::Time::now()); ros::spin(); Papers cite repository/package, cite book chapter (PDF Springer Link) Ralph Lange, Silvio Traversaro, Oliver Lenord, Christian Bertsch: Integrating Functional Mock-Up Interface ROS Gazebo. description: ROS Foxglove Bridge
readme: foxglove_bridge High performance ROS ROS WebSocket bridge Foxglove WebSocket protocol, written Motivation Live debugging ROS systems traditionally relied running ROS tooling rviz. requires GUI connected peripherals robot, replicating ROS environment network-connected development machine including version ROS, custom message definitions, overcome limitation remote debugging web tooling non-ROS systems, rosbridge developed. rosbridge suffers performance problems high frequency topics and/or large messages, protocol support full visibility ROS systems interacting parameters full graph publishers subscribers. foxglove_bridge Foxglove WebSocket protocol, similar protocol rosbridge ability support additional schema formats ROS .msg ROS .idl, parameters, graph introspection, non-ROS systems. bridge written designed high performance overhead minimize impact robot stack. Installation Note: project active development binary releases foxglove_bridge outdated. latest features bug fixes, building foxglove_bridge source. foxglove_bridge package ROS Melodic Noetic, ROS Humble Rolling. Earlier releases ROS supported due API design and/or performance limitations. package installed command: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-foxglove-bridge Running bridge run bridge node, recommended launch file: ROS roslaunch --screen foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge.launch port:=8765 <launch> Including launch file <include file="$(find foxglove_bridge)/launch/foxglove_bridge.launch"> <arg name="port" arguments </include> </launch> ROS ros2 launch foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge_launch.xml port:=8765 <launch> Including launch file <include file="$(find-pkg-share foxglove_bridge)/launch/foxglove_bridge_launch.xml"> <arg name="port" arguments </include> </launch> Configuration Parameters configure behavior bridge. parameters set initialization launch file command line, modified runtime. port: TCP port bind WebSocket server valid TCP port number, random port. Defaults address: host address bind WebSocket server Defaults listening interfaces default. Change IPv6) accept connections local machine. tls: true, Transport Layer Security (TLS) encrypted communication. Defaults false. certfile: Path certificate TLS. Required tls set true. Defaults keyfile: Path private key TLS. Required tls set true. Defaults topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted topic names. Defaults service_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted service names. Defaults param_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted parameter names. Defaults client_topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) whitelisted client-published topic names. Defaults send_buffer_limit: Connection send buffer limit bytes. Messages dropped connection’s send buffer reaches limit avoid queue outdated messages building Defaults use_compression: websocket compression (permessage-deflate). recommended leave turned increases CPU usage per-message compression yields compression ratios robotics data. Defaults false. capabilities: List supported server capabilities. Defaults [clientPublish,parameters,parametersSubscribe,services,connectionGraph,assets]. asset_uri_allowlist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript grammar) allowed asset URIs. resource_retriever resolve package://, file:// http(s):// URIs. Note list carefully configured confidential files accidentally exposed websocket connection. extra security measure, URIs consecutive dots disallowed construct URIs retrieval confidential files allowlist configured strict package://<pkg_name>/../../../secret.txt). Defaults ["^package://(?:[-\w]+/)*[-\w]+\.(?:dae|fbx|glb|gltf|jpeg|jpg|mtl|obj|png|stl|tif|tiff|urdf|webp|xacro)$"]. (ROS max_update_ms: maximum number milliseconds wait polling roscore topics, services, parameters. Defaults (ROS service_type_retrieval_timeout_ms: Max number milliseconds retrieving services type information. Defaults (ROS num_threads: number threads ROS node executor. controls number subscriptions processed parallel. means thread CPU core. Defaults (ROS min_qos_depth: Minimum depth QoS profile subscriptions. Defaults set limit subscriber’s QoS depth computed summing depths publishers. (ROS max_qos_depth: Maximum depth QoS profile subscriptions. Defaults (ROS best_effort_qos_topic_whitelist: List regular expressions (ECMAScript) topics forced ‘best_effort’ QoS. Unmatched topics ‘reliable’ QoS publishers ‘reliable’, ‘best_effort’ publishers ‘best_effort’. Defaults (match (ROS include_hidden: Include hidden topics services. Defaults false. (ROS disable_load_message: publish loaned message publishing client message. Defaults true. (ROS ignore_unresponsive_param_nodes: Avoid requesting parameters previously unresponsive nodes. Defaults true. Building source Fetch source install dependencies <path/to/your/ros_ws> git clone https://github.com/foxglove/ros-foxglove-bridge.git src/ros-foxglove-bridge rosdep update rosdep install --ignore-src --default-yes --from-path src ROS catkin_make source install/local_setup.bash roslaunch --screen foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge.launch ROS colcon build --event-handlers console_direct+ --symlink-install source install/local_setup.bash ros2 launch foxglove_bridge foxglove_bridge_launch.xml Clients Foxglove connects foxglove_bridge live robotics visualization. Development VSCode container dual ROS ROS installation tools build run bridge. bash aliases defined simplify common workflow. Here’s building running ROS node: source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash ros2_build_debug ros2_build_release ros2_foxglove_bridge test bridge data, open terminal download test .mcap files: ./download_test_data.sh start playback: source /opt/ros/humble/setup.bash ros2 bag play --clock mcap data/nuScenes-v1.0-mini-scene-0061-ros2.mcap License foxglove_bridge released MIT license. full terms conditions, LICENSE file.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

    point in time.
