description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.
hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.
hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt
hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

Common code for working with images in ROS.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.
maintainers: Vincent Rabaud, Steven Macenski, Autonomoustuff team

A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a

    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into a quaternion to represent the orientation of the device wrt the global frame. Based on the algorithm by Roberto G. Valenti etal. described in the paper "Keeping a Good Attitude: A Quaternion-Based Orientation Filter for IMUs and MARGs" available at http://www.mdpi.com/1424-8220/15/8/19302 .

Filter which fuses angular velocities, accelerations, and (optionally) magnetic readings from a generic IMU device into an orientation. Based on code by Sebastian Madgwick, http://www.x-io.co.uk/node/8#open_source_ahrs_and_imu_algorithms.

description:
IRIS package for Localization and Mapping (LaMa).
        This packages includes the sparse-dense library for grid mapping,
        a 2D localization algorithm based on scan matching, an online SLAM solution
        with a dynamic likelihood field and a 2D RB Particle Filter SLAM solution
        with multithreading.
readme: LaMa Localization Mapping library. https://github.com/iris-ua/iris_lama Developed maintained Eurico Pedrosa, University Aveiro Overview LaMa software library robotic localization mapping developed Intelligent Robotics Systems (IRIS) Laboratory University Aveiro Portugal. includes framework volumetric grids mapping), localization algorithm based scan matching SLAM solution Online SLAM Particle Filter SLAM). main feature efficiency. computational effort memory usage minimum viable computer run localization SLAM solutions Raspberry Model Build build LaMa, clone GitHub CMake build. git clone https://github.com/iris-ua/iris_lama iris_lama mkdir build build cmake dependency Eigen3. Note: LaMa provide executable. integration ROS. Integration ROS source code package.xml library external ros packages. provide ROS nodes run localization SLAM solutions. iris_lama_ros information. Sparse-Dense Mapping (SDM) Sparse-Dense Mapping (SDM) framework efficient implementation volumetric grids. divides space small dense patches addressable sparse data-structure. improve memory usage individual patch compressed live operations lossless data compression Zstandard) overhead. replacement OctoMap. grid maps implemented: Distance Map: distance closest occupied cells map. provide DynamicDistanceMap implementation dynamic Euclidean map proposed Lau, Sprunk, Burgard Efficient Grid-Based Spatial Representations Robot Navigation Dynamic Environments Robotics Autonomous Systems, Elsevier Occupancy Map: common representation environment robotics. variants occupancy map SimpleOccupancyMap cell tri-state: free, occupied unknown: ProbabilisticOccupancyMap encodes occupancy probability cell logods; FrequencyOccupancyMap tracks number times beam hits traverses cell calculates hit/miss ratio. information SDM read Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Sparse-Dense Approach Efficient Grid Mapping IEEE International Conference Autonomous Robot Systems Competitions (ICARSC) Localization based Scan Matching provide fast scan matching approach mobile robot localization supported continuous likelihood field. provide accurate localization robots equipped laser good odometry. good odometry recommended. Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Efficient Localization Based Scan Matching Continuous Likelihood Field IEEE International Conference Autonomous Robot Systems Competitions (ICARSC) Online SLAM environments considerable loops solution accurate efficient. run real time low-spec computer running turtlebot raspberry localization algorithm combined dynamic likelihood field incrementally build occupancy map. information read Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Non-Linear Squares Approach SLAM Dynamic Likelihood Field Journal Intelligent Robotic Systems Multi-threaded Particle Filter SLAM Particle Filter SLAM RBPF SLAM GMapping extension Online SLAM solution multiple particles multi-thread support. solution capable parallelizing localization mapping processes. thread-pool manage number working threads. multi-threading, solutions lightweight competitor heavyweight GMapping. information read Eurico Pedrosa, Artur Pereira, Nuno Lau Fast Grid SLAM Based Particle Filter Scan Matching Multithreading IEEE International Conference Autonomous Robot Systems Competitions (ICARSC), Ponta Delgada, Portugal, doi: 10.1109/ICARSC49921.2020.9096191. description:
Mesh Conversion Utility

Used to generate '.iv' files from '.stl' files.  This package has not
been changed since 2001 and appears to be very stable.  We plan on
keeping this package in this revision for mesh conversions.  This
package is only available as a single source file for download.  There
are no local modifications to this package.

description:
ROS driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend
readme: ROS Driver Generic Linux Joysticks joy package joy_node, node interfaces generic Linux joystick ROS. node publishes “Joy” message, current state joystick’s buttons axes. Supported Hardware node work joystick supported Linux. Published Topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy): outputs joystick state. Device Selection parameters controlling device ~dev (string, default: “/dev/input/js0”) ~dev_name (string, default: (empty disabled)) ~dev_name empty, ~dev defines Linux joystick device read joystick events. ~dev_name defined, node enumerates joysticks, /dev/input/js*. joystick matching ~dev_name opened. joystick matches desired device device ~dev fallback. list names connected joysticks, invalid ~dev_name rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="*" output INFO]: joystick: LIS3LV02DL Accelerometer (/dev/input/js1). INFO]: joystick: Microsoft X-Box pad (/dev/input/js0). [ERROR]: find joystick Falling default device. node started device list. rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="Microsoft X-Box pad" Advanced Parameters ~deadzone (double, default: Amount joystick move considered off-center. parameter relative axis normalized means joystick move edge axis’s range axis output non-zero Linux deadzone processing, cases set ~autorepeat_rate (double, default: (disabled)) Rate joystick non-changing state resend previously message. ~coalesce_interval (double, default: Axis events received coalesce_interval (seconds) single ROS message. kernel sends axis motion separate event, coalescing greatly reduces rate messages option limit rate outgoing messages. Button events immediately avoid missing button presses. Information information Wiki page.

description:
This metapackage depends on packages for interfacing common
    joysticks and human input devices with ROS.

Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.
    Driver for the Sony PlayStation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3
    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens
    for a connection on the HID ports, starts the joystick
    streaming data, and passes the data to the Linux uinput device
    so that it shows up as a normal joystick.
authors: Blaise Gassend, pascal <AT> pabr.org, Melonee Wise

description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to

Pub/Sub mode set rosparams receiving_mode=pubsub, google_cloud_credentials_json, project_id, subscription_id. subscription_id chat-sub follow Pub/Sub mode roslaunch google_chat_ros google_chat.launch receiving_mode:=pubsub google_cloud_credentials_json:=/path/to/<project_id>-XXXXXXXX.json project_id:=<project_id> subscription_id:=chat-sub Sending message Understanding Google Chat Room Google Chat browsers smartphone’s apps, space, thread. send message, space thread send message space chat room’s URL. https://mail.google.com/chat/u/0/#chat/space/XXXXXXXXXXX, XXXXXXXXXXX space Message format types messages, text card. card basically original json structure. node covers units ros action msgs, complicated Examples sections, show simple Sending message actionlib send Actionlib goal ~send/goal. description: A simple viewer for ROS image topics with draw-on features
readme: image_view2 image_view2 extended image_view. Features Draw markers image view Interact user direction Interaction Modes image_view2 support mode interact user. set interaction mode ~interaction_mode parameter change mode service interfaces. Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Polygon mode (~interaction_mode:=poly) polygon mode, user select series closed lines. User add line left-click close lines right-click. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut) grabcut mode, user select curves. typing Esc key, clear selected curves. selected red curve published image/foreground (sensor_msgs/Image) green publiehd image/background (sensor_msgs/Image). mode originally implemented select forground background pixels grabcut. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. ROS API Publising Topics image/marked (sensor_msgs/Image) Image marks drawed. image/screenrectangle_image (sensor_msgs/Image) Cropped image user selection Rectangle mode. Subscribing Topics image (sensor_msgs/Image) Input image camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) CameraInfo image. camera info required compute projection matrix drawing markers. image_marker (image_view2/ImageMarker2) Marker topic. image/event (image_view2/MouseEvent) Event interface. image_view2 handle mouse events gui interaction. topic emulate event handling ros topics Advertising Services ~change_mode (image_view2/ChangeMode) Service interface change interaction mode. ~rectangle_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~grabcut_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~grabcut_rect_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~line_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~poly_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~none_mode (std_srvs/Empty) Service interface change interaction mode std_srvs/Empty service. image_view2/ImageMarker2 image_view2/ImageMarker2 message draw image_view2 canvas. readme: jsk_rosbag_tools Tools creating video rosbag compressing rosbag images. bag_to_video.py Create video rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_video.py [--fps FPS] [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] [--image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC input_bagfile rosbag video positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output directory path filename. --image-topic interpreted directory file --fps FPS --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC --image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC Topic extract. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_video.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio --image-topic /head_camera/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color/compressed /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.mp4 bag_to_audio.py Create audio file rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_audio.py [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] input_bagfile rosbag audio positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output filename. `--audio-topic`_info exists, samplerate channels. --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_audio.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.wav video_to_bag.py Convert video file bagfile. The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

description: The jsk_calibration package
readme: jsk_calibration utility package hand/eye calibration ros-perception/calibration. package generates sampling poses automaticaly euslisp robot models. robot documentation HRP2JSKNTS Calibrate robot calibration process steps. capture data. Move robot capturing data bag file. roslaunch capture_data.launch estimate parameters. Estimate calibration parameter bag file. ./calibrate_${ROBOT}.sh calibrated urdf file. check progress estimation parameters rviz: rosrun rviz rviz view_results/pose_guesses.rviz calibration takes lot time, initial_pose.yaml. Update system.yaml adding initial_poses: /path/to/initial_pose.yaml. description: A metapackage to bring in the default packages second generation Transform Library in ros, tf2.
The tf2_py package for python3 compatibility in melodic environment. Supported by ROS community.
tf2 unit tests

tf2 is the second generation of the transform library, which lets
    the user keep track of multiple coordinate frames over time. tf2
    maintains the relationship between coordinate frames in a tree
    structure buffered in time, and lets the user transform points,
    vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at any desired
    point in time.

description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. script convert multiple message types spectrum. Publishing topics audio_to_spectrum.py. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data convert spectrum published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample input data FFT. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. spectrum_filter.py script filter spectrum. Publishing topics ~output (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Filtered spectrum data (low_cut_freq-high_cut_freq). Subscribing topics ~input (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Original spectrum data. Parameters ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate data generation original spectrum data. ~high_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit maximum frequency output spectrum. ~low_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit minimum frequency output spectrum. spectrum_to_spectrogram.py script convert spectrum spectrogram. Publishing topics ~spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image) Spectrogram data, concatenation spectrum time series. Image format Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~image_height (Int, default: Number vertical (frequency axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~image_width (Int, default: Number horizontal (time axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~spectrogram_period (Double, default: Period store spectrum data create spectrogram topic. ~publish_rate (Double, default: image_width spectrogram_period) Publish rate spectrogram topic. audio_amplitude_plot.py script publish audio amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Audio amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size sound input plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate audio amplitude image topic. data_amplitude_plot.py Generalized version audio_amplitude_plot.py. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Data amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data amplitude plot published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size input data plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate data amplitude image topic. spectrum_plot.py script publish frequency amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Frequency amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~min_amp (Double, default: Minimum amplitude plot. ~max_amp (Double, default: Maximum amplitude plot. ~queue_size (Int, default: Queue size spectrum subscriber. ~max_rate (Double, default: Maximum publish rate frequency amplitude plot image. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Save environmental noise train_data/noise.npy. subtracting noise, spectrograms clear. script, give sound sensor. update noise data everytime sound recognition, environmental sound differs everytime. noise samples roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Publish audio spectrum spectrogram topics. set max/min frequency included spectrum high_cut_freq/low_cut_freq args audio_to_spectrogram.launch. gui:=true, spectrum spectrogram visualized. roslaunch sound_classification audio_to_spectrogram.launch gui:=true spectrogram quiet environment. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. Rosbag version (Recommended) recommend rosbag collect spectrograms. rosbag easy save_sound.launch parameters. target_class:=TARGET_CLASS, set class target sound. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG, save spectrograms rosbag. default, rosbag paused Press ‘Space’ key terminal start playing rosbag. rosbag ends, press ‘Ctrl-c’ terminate. newly saved spectrograms appended existing spectrograms. change threshold sound saving threshold:=xxx. smaller easily sound saved. Save audio rosbag roslaunch sound_classification record_audio_rosbag.launch filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG play rosbag collecting data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 setting `threshold:=0` `save_when_sound:=false`, collect spectrogram sound". play rosbag collecting no-sound data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false Stream version Recommended) collect spectrogram audio topic stream. `use_rosbag:=true`. args rosbag version. roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch save_when_sound:=true target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 save_data_rate:=5 Create dateaset chainer saved spectrograms. data augmentation executed. --number means maximum images class dataset. rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Visualize dataset. train arg train dataset (augmented dataset), test arg test dataset. spectrograms dataset visualized random order. rosrun sound_classification visualize_dataset.py test train/test Train dataset. Default model NIN (Recommended). vgg16, pretrained weights VGG16 downloaded scripts/VGG_ILSVRC_16_layers.npz time run script. rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sounds. takes seconds neural network weights loaded. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG classify sound rosbag. roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch fix class names' color classification result image order class names <rosparam> target_names: chip_bag] </rosparam> classification result: |no_sound|applause|voice| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828259-ed487980-c0cd-11ea-9f51-7ccb0bc93321.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828249-ec174c80-c0cd-11ea-854c-da1b0fa08e33.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828260-ede11000-c0cd-11ea-9b4e-94ee7b1c1a5f.png)| Quick demo Sound classification demo laptop’s built-in microphone. create dataset rosbag files sample_rosbag/ directory. Classification gif Commands Setup environment write Microphone settings Usage section) Save environmental noise roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Collect spectrograms sample rosbags. Press ‘Space’ start rosbag. no_sound class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/no_sound.bag target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false applause class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/applause.bag target_class:=applause threshold:=0.5 voice class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/voice.bag target_class:=voice threshold:=0.5 Create dataset rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Train (takes minites) rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sound roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch

Setting means waiting euslisp simulation mongodb, recommended set avoid stuck. json(bson) related parameters *json-parse-object-as* (:alist :plist, default: :alist) Destination type evaluating json object *json-parse-key-function* (#'identity, #'string->keyword functions, default: #'string->keyword) Destination type key evaluation json object euslisp/mongo-client-sample.l roseus json Parse json string load "package://roseus_mongo/euslisp/json/json-decode.l" setq *parsed* (json::parse-from-string "{\"key1\" \"key2\" parse json-string assoc :key1 *parsed* (:key1 car (assoc :key1 *parsed*) :key1 cdr (assoc :key1 *parsed*) Convert lisp-alist json string load "package://roseus_mongo/euslisp/json/json-encode.l" setq *alist* '(("key1" ("key2" json::stream->string #'json::encode-alist *alist* "{\"key1\":\"value1\",\"key2\":\"value2\"}"

description:
roseus_smach

     * Euslisp state machine class. it will be moved.
     * Message publisher for visualizing current state by smach_viewer.
     * Simple pickle dump script for debugging state machine.
     * Execute state machine as a action server.
readme: roseus_smach package includes euslisp implementation state machine smach. requirements roseus smach smach_viewer Optional visualization. install package manually: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-smach-viewer sample Sample codes sample directory. rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l simple state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-simple) nested state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-nested) state machine userdata rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-userdata) sample/parallel-state-machine-sample.l state machine parallel action execution rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach parallel-state-machine-sample.l (demo) Writing Simple Smach(state-machine) codes #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (setq count (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) (defun smach-simple (instance state-machine :init))) (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) (send :add-node (instance state :init :BAR 'func-bar)) goal-states generated method (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) select node start-node (send :start-state :FOO) nodes selected symbol (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) (send :add-transition :FOO :outcome4 :outcome2) (send :add-transition :BAR :FOO :outcome2) (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) Code Explained (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) lines define functions hooked state. Note functions called argument, alist declared user-data arguments, return equal transition action) state. (instance state-machine :init)) line creates state-machine instance binds (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) line creates state add node state machine. create state instance, state hook state function. lisp object state names. case, state :FOO hooked func-foo. (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) (send :start-state :FOO) lines define goal state(s) start state(s) state machine. create multiple start states list state, define multiple goal state. Goal state(s) returns Note state machine state. goal state considered outcome state machine. (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) line defines transition. arguments :add-transition method OUTCOME. line means “adding transition FOO BAR, FOO node returns outcome1”. (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) line sets execute state machine. order view check progress smach_viewer, function exec-state-machine Writing Nested Smach codes add child state-machine node state-machine. #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (defun smach-nested ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) state instance include state-machine function (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) node instance args :add-node, :start-state, :add-transition ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) sm-top (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Code Explained #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") previous line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) line creates state-machine instance. sm-sub state machine acting state node) sm-top. easy understand state machine function: state machine called, executed, state machine processing eventually returns goal state return function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) sm-sub instance hooked node sm-top line. line add state machine state function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) lines define node, goal-state, start-state, transition sm-top. (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) Remember goal state(s) state-machine returns outcome return sm-sub goal state(s). goal state sm-sub :outcome4, return sm-sub :outcome4. line adds transition :outcome5 node returns :outcome4. ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) lines define behavior sm-sub detail previous simple state machine Note (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) defines goal state, defines return state machine. (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Finally, sm-top executed Writing Simple Smach (make-state-machine) make-state-machine function easy-way define simple state machine. requires graph-list, func-map, initial-state, goal-states arguments. simple state machine written (defun smach-simple2 (setq (make-state-machine define graph, list (<from-node> <transition> <to-node>) <transition> corresponds node returns nil. '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) define function map '((:foo 'func-foo) foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2 (:bar 'func-bar)) bar returns :outcome2 initial state '(:foo) goal state '(:outcome4))))) node :foo :bar :outcome4 terminate node. node corresponds 'func-foo 'func-bar functions. function 'func-foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2. function 'func-bar returns :outcome2. (:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) means :foo returns :outcome2, transit :outcome4. (:foo :outcome1 :bar) means :foo returns :outcome1, transit :bar. (:bar :outcome2 :foo) means :bar returns :outcome2, transit :foo. simplify state machine definition, recommend users t/nil return node, users able (:foo :outcome4) graph definition. (defun smach-simple3 (setq (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome4) (:foo :bar) (:bar :foo)) '((:foo '(lambda (&rest args) (cond count (incf count) nil) foo returns nil times returns (:bar '(lambda (&rest args) bar returns '(:foo) '(:outcome4))))) tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple2)))" roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple3)))" check state machine behavior rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py` Writing Nested Smach (make-state-machine) write nested state machine make-state-machie function. add state-machine function maps, added node (defun smach-simple-nested (sm-top sm-sub) (setq sm-sub (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) transitions (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) '((:foo 'func-foo) function maps (:bar 'func-bar)) '(:foo) initial '(:outcome4) goal (setq sm-top (make-state-machine '((:bas :outcome3 transitions :outcome4 :outcome5)) `((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps ,sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" '(:bas) initial '(:outcome5) goal sm-top)) roseus write '((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" careful add machine. `(backquote) (comma). tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple-nested)))"

Integrate robot’s perception query objects task planning system easily manage facts. Persistent storage facts map geometry backed local PostgreSQL database Convenient APIs common queries, instances type object Link code, import Python binding dependent ROS, easy integrate ROS projects Representation Knowledge broken concepts, instances attributes. Attributes tie entities store data attribute type. apple instance instance. attribute named is_on type entity_id, relates entity Attributes type entity_id called relations relate entities Attributes types entity_id function properties; relation is_graspable type bool storing simple fact entity. A controller ensuring the safe operation of Kobuki.

    The SafetyController keeps track of bumper, cliff and wheel drop events. In case of the first two,
    Kobuki is commanded to move back. In the latter case, Kobuki is stopped.
    
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (bumper pressed etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!

Connects serial KVH GEO FOG device publishes KVH-specific generic ROS-ified messages, performs basic transforms convert data ROS-conformant conventions. description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages
ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.
authors: Tully Foote, Radu Bogdan Rusu, William Woodall

description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.
This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.
The linux_networking package
sdsdddsdsds

Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.
A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.
Simple script to check battery status
A ROS node for using libsensors to provide diagnostics information about the sensors on a computer system.

Simple scripts which help utilise, monitor, interact with computer
     hardware abstracted by a linux OS.
The lms1xx package contains a basic ROS driver for the SICK LMS1xx line of LIDARs.

description: Library for interfacing to USB devices

The Las Vegas Surface Reconstruction Toolkit is an Open Source toolkit to reconstruct triangle meshes from unordered point clouds. It supports the generation of textured models either using colored point clouds or point clouds plus images and according calibration matrices. The LVR toolkit provides an Open Source C++ API for meshing and texture generation as well as an I/O interface to store the generated meshes in different exchange formats including Stanford PLY, Wavefront OBJ and Collada. In contrast to other meshing software, it focuses on reconstruction of large scale environments on city scale from high resolution point clouds.

The marker_msgs package contains messages usable to setup a marker/fiducial system. 
    The package distinguishes between two types of messages. 
    First messages to describe the properties of a marker/fiducial detection system and the detected markers. 
    Secondly messages used to represent a map of markers/features with covariances as it would be produced by a SLAM system or published by a map server for self-localization.

description: The mbf_recovery_behaviors package

The Move Base Flex (MBF) recovery behavior moves the robot back for specified length.
        It also checks the costmap for a possible collision behind the robot while moving and
        stops the robot if necessary.
readme: Moveback recovery simple recovery behavior moves robot implements move base flex recovery interface. Parameters control_frequency cycle frequency executing break checks, (default: linear_vel_back velocity driving robot (default: m/sec) step_back_length distance move robot (default: step_back_timeout timeout stopping robot, (default: sec) footprint_inflation footprint inflation check costmap obstacles, (default: look_behind_dist robot pose checking distance costmap footprint checks, (default: publish_back_point Publishes point distance robot footprint position point, (default: false) control_frequency cycle frequency executing break checks, (default: linear_vel_back velocity driving robot (default: m/sec) step_back_length distance move robot (default: step_back_timeout timeout stopping robot, (default: sec) footprint_inflation footprint inflation check costmap obstacles, (default: look_behind_dist robot pose checking distance costmap footprint checks, (default: publish_back_point Publishes point distance robot footprint position point, (default: false)

description: 3-D/6-DOF localization for mobile robots with 3-D LIDAR(s)
readme: mcl_3dl Package summary mcl_3dl ROS node perform probabilistic 3-D/6-DOF localization system mobile robots LIDAR(s). implements pointcloud based Monte Carlo localization reference pointcloud map. node receives reference pointcloud environment map localizes 6-DOF yaw, pitch, roll) pose measured pointclouds assisted motion prediction odometry. supported motion model differential-wheeled-robot. node classic MCL; doesn’t implement adaptive feature KDL-sampling Algorithms fundamental algorithm mcl_3dl node Monte Carlo localization (MCL), aka particle filter localization. MCL represents probabilistic distribution estimated pose density weight particles estimates pose distribution. Algorithm details Parameters Node Install source Note: mcl_3dl_msgs package required build mcl_3dl package. clone /path/to/your/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl.git git clone https://github.com/at-wat/mcl_3dl_msgs.git build /path/to/your/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src Install dependencies catkin_make -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release Release build recommended apt repository ROS Indigo/Kinetic/Lunar Ubuntu) sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-mcl-3dl Running demo bag file 2+4-DOF tracked vehicle Hokuyo YVT-X002 LIDAR online. Pre-processed (filtered) pointcloud, IMU pose, odometry, map data packed bag. Download bag wget ~/Downloads https://openspur.org/~atsushi.w/dataset/mcl_3dl/short_test3.bag Running demo roslaunch mcl_3dl test.launch use_pointcloud_map:=false use_cad_map:=false use_bag_file:=true bag_file:=${HOME}/Downloads/short_test3.bag map data bag generated cartographer_ros filtered pcl_outlier_removal pcl_voxel_grid utilities. MarkerArray shows mcl_3dl internal information. Purple spheres: sampled points likelihood-model calculation Red lines: casted rays beam-model calculation Red boxes: detected collisions raycasting global localization, call /global_localization command. rosservice call /global_localization Demos odometry IMU Contributing mcl_3dl package developed GitHub flow. Feel free open Issue and/or Pull Request. code repository ROS Style Guide. configuration file clang-format https://github.com/seqsense/ros_style/. License mcl_3dl BSD license. Backport codes Point Cloud Library (PCL) BSD license.

This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the AbstractPlanner, AbstractController and AbstractRecovery plugin interfaces. This interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make the plugin available for Move Base Flex. The abstract classes provides a meaningful interface enabling the planners, controllers and recovery behaviors to return information, e.g. why something went wrong. Derivided interfaces can, for example, provide methods to initialize the planner, controller or recovery with map representations like costmap_2d, grid_map or other representations.

Derived packages mbf_abstract_nav derived interfaces mbf_abstract_core method initialization plugin, handing pointer map representation choice. plugins loaded Move Base Flex derived package mbf_abstract_nav) implement derived interfaces interfaces defined package. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractPlanner interface planner plugin plan global paths. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractController interface controller plugin control move robot. mbf_abstract_core::AbstractRecovery interface recovery plugin recover robot deadlocked situations.

description: The mbf_abstract_nav package contains the abstract navigation server implementation of Move Base Flex (MBF). The abstract navigation server is not bound to any map representation. It provides the actions for planning, controlling and recovering. MBF loads all defined plugins at the program start. Therefor, it loads all plugins which are defined in the lists *planners*, *controllers* and *recovery_behaviors*. Each list holds a pair of a *name* and a *type*. The *type* defines which kind of plugin to load. The *name* defines under which name the plugin should be callable by the actions.
readme: Move Base Flex Abstract Navigation Server mbf_abstract_nav package abstract navigation server implementation Move Base Flex (MBF). abstract navigation server bound map representation. Actions planning, controlling recovering. time start MBF loads defined plugins. Therefor, loads plugins defined lists planners, controllers recovery_behaviors. list holds pair type. type defines kind plugin load. defines plugin callable actions.

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. It contains the CostmapPlanner, CostmapController and CostmapRecovery interfaces. The interfaces have to be implemented by the plugins to make them available for Move Base Flex using the mbf_costmap_nav navigation implementation. That implementation inherits the mbf_abstract_nav implementation and binds the system to a local and a global costmap.
readme: Move Base Flex Costmap Navigation Core package common interfaces navigation specific robot actions. CostmapPlanner, CostmapController CostmapRecovery interfaces. interfaces implemented plugins Move Base Flex mbf_costmap_nav navigation implementation. implementation inherits mbf_abstract_nav implementation binds system local global costmap.

description: The mbf_utility package

Move Base Flex (MBF) is a backwards-compatible replacement for move_base. MBF can use existing plugins for move_base, and provides an enhanced version of the planner, controller and recovery plugin ROS interfaces. It exposes action servers for planning, controlling and recovering, providing detailed information of the current state and the plugin’s feedback. An external executive logic can use MBF and its actions to perform smart and flexible navigation strategies. Furthermore, MBF enables the use of other map representations, e.g. meshes or grid_map
       
       This package is a meta package and refers to the Move Base Flex stack packages.The abstract core of MBF – without any binding to a map representation – is represented by the
The move_base_sequence package

description: ROS package for Multiple objects detection, tracking and classification from LIDAR scans/point-clouds
readme: Multiple objects detection, tracking classification LIDAR scans/point-clouds PCL based ROS package Detect/Cluster Track Classify static dynamic objects real-time LIDAR scans implemented Features: tree based point cloud processing object feature detection point clouds Unsupervised euclidean cluster extraction k-means clustering based detected features refinement RANSAC Stable tracking (object data association) ensemble Kalman Filters Robust compared k-means clustering mean-flow tracking Usage: Follow steps (multi_object_tracking_lidar) package: Create catkin workspace setup Navigate src folder catkin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src Clone repository: git clone https://github.com/praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar.git Compile build package: ~/catkin_ws catkin_make Add catkin workspace ROS environment: source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Run kf_tracker ROS node package: rosrun multi_object_tracking_lidar kf_tracker ROS node running! long point clouds published filtered_cloud rostopic, outputs node published obj_id, cluster_0, cluster_1, cluster_5 topics markers topic visualize RViz. Supported point-cloud streams/sources: input point-clouds real LiDAR simulated LiDAR point cloud dataset data source produces point clouds Note: package expects valid point cloud data input. point clouds publish “filtered_cloud” expected NaNs. point cloud filtering task application dependent module. PCL library pcl::removeNaNFromPointCloud method filter NaN points. refer code snippet easily filter NaN points point cloud. Citing code snippets repository work, cite: @software{praveen_palanisamy_2019_3559187, author {Praveen Palanisamy}, title {{praveen-palanisamy/multiple-object-tracking-lidar: Multiple-Object-Tracking-from-Point-Clouds_v1.0.2}}, month dec, year publisher {Zenodo}, version doi {10.5281/zenodo.3559187}, url {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3559186} Wiki Checkout Wiki pages Multiple-object tracking pointclouds Velodyne VLP-16

description:
The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description: This planner attempts to find a legal place to put a carrot for the robot to follow. It does this by moving back along the vector between the robot and the goal point.

description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
This package provides an implementation of the Dynamic Window Approach to
        local robot navigation on a plane. Given a global plan to follow and a
        costmap, the local planner produces velocity commands to send to a mobile
        base. This package supports any robot who's footprint can be represented as
        a convex polygon or cicrle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters
        that can be set in a launch file. The parameters for this planner are also
        dynamically reconfigurable. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the
        BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor
        streams, and a goal pose and outputs safe velocity commands that are sent
        to a mobile base.
maintainers: Michael Ferguson, David V. Lu!!, Aaron Hoy
readme: ROS Navigation Stack navigation stack takes information odometry, sensor streams, goal pose outputs safe velocity commands mobile base. Related stacks: http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_tutorials http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_experimental

description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy

description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Aaron Hoy
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.
    Includes OpenKarto GraphSLAM library by "SRI International".

Wrapper around Particle Filter implementation.
    The SelfLocalizer can be used as library or as a ros-node.
Messages used for 2D-Navigation.

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.
Contains a set of tutorials that run 2D-Navigation within Stage-Simulator.

The assisted_teleop node subscribes to a desired trajectory topic
    (geometry_msgs/Twist) and uses TrajectoryPlannerROS to find a valid
    trajectory close to the desired trajectory before republishing. Useful for
    filtering teleop commands while avoiding obstacles. This package also
    contains LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, which is a LaserScan filter plugin that
    takes max range values in a scan and turns them into valid values that are
    slightly less than max range.
readme: assisted_teleop assisted_teleop node subscribes desired trajectory topic (geometry_msgs/Twist) TrajectoryPlannerROS find valid trajectory close desired trajectory republishing. filtering teleop commands avoiding obstacles. package LaserScanMaxRangeFilter, LaserScan filter plugin takes max range values scan turns valid values slightly max range.

description:
The sbpl_lattice_planner is a global planner plugin for move_base and wraps
    the SBPL search-based planning library.
readme: sbpl_lattice_planner sbpl_lattice_planner global planner plugin move_base wraps SBPL search-based planning library. Paths generated combining series “motion primitives” short, kinematically feasible motions. Planning theta dimensions, resulting smooth paths robot orientation account, important robot assumed circular nonholonomic constraints robot move sideways). Plans ARA* planner planner SBPL library. Robot developers simple defining hard-coded mappings natural language commands robot instructions, complex small-scale NLP engine, BINS supports definitions synonyms, phrases ngram), RegEx word patterns. supports nesting patterns, enabling robot developers build hierarchical patterns. matching pre-defined pattern, BINS lets developer produce output plain text, robot executable instructions, intents, entities, json format. nmc_nlp_lite, wrapper BINS, launched ROS node supports publish/subscribe mode asynchronous access) service mode synchronous access). description: Object_recognition_msgs contains the ROS message and the actionlib definition used in object_recognition_core

The dynamicEDT3D library implements an inrementally updatable Euclidean distance transform (EDT) in 3D. It comes with a wrapper to use the OctoMap 3D representation and hooks into the change detection of the OctoMap library to propagate changes to the EDT.

The OctoMap library implements a 3D occupancy grid mapping approach, providing data structures and mapping algorithms in C++. The map implementation is based on an octree. See
  http://octomap.github.io for details.
readme: Octomap probabilistic, flexible, compact mapping library robotic systems Authors: Kai Wurm Armin Hornung, University Freiburg, Copyright https://octomap.github.io list contributors authors. License octomap: BSD License REQUIREMENTS octomap library: cmake regular build environment (gcc) HTML documentation: doxygen (optional) viewer octovis: OpenGL, QGLViewer (optional) Skip WINDOWS tips compilation Windows. install dependencies Ubuntu running: sudo apt-get install cmake doxygen libqt4-dev libqt4-opengl-dev libqglviewer-dev-qt4 (Note: older releases Ubuntu exchange package libqglviewer-qt4-dev) INSTALLATION http://www.ros.org/wiki/octomap OctoMap ROS! pre-compiled packages octomap, octovis, ROS integration Build complete project changing “build” directory running cmake: mkdir build build cmake Type compile create CMake files cleanly build folder (Out-of-source build). Executables bin, libraries lib. debug configuration created running: cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug build directory build-debug). install library running install, adjust CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX target test executes unit tests octomap library, interested verifying functionality machine. DOCUMENTATION documentation latest stable release online: https://octomap.github.io/octomap/doc/index.html build current HTML-Documentation current source Doxygen running docs build directory. documentation doc/html/index.html main directory. readme: ProAut OctoMap Introduction package designed automatically remove outdated voxels original octomap. motivation concept octomap-website find supportive videos. explanations, workshop abstract. Nodes implementation decay: rosrun octomap_pa octree_stamped_pa_node roslaunch octomap_pa octomap_stamped_pa.launch native implementation decay original octomap package: rosrun octomap_pa octree_stamped_native_node roslaunch octomap_pa octomap_stamped_native.launch Simple node decay: rosrun octomap_pa octree_pa_node roslaunch octomap_pa octomap_pa.launch Input Output Topics Topic Type Description ”~/in_cloud” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Input pointcloud type. ”~/in_cloud_old” sensor_msgs/PointCloud Input pointloud type. converted pointcloud type. ”~/in_laser” sensor_msgs/LaserScan Input single laser scan. converted pointcloud type package “laser_geometry”. ”~/out_octomap” octomap_msgs/Octomap Output binary octomap voxels free occupied (smaller size). ”~/out_octomap_full” octomap_msgs/Octomap Output octomap (full size). ”~/out_cloud_free” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Output free voxels pointcloud. ”~/out_cloud_occupied” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Output occupied voxels pointcloud. topics remapped parameters Services Service Type Description ”~/clear” std_srvs/Empty Deletes internal octomap. ”~/reset” octomap_pa_msgs/Reset resolution frame clearing internal octomap. ”~/getsize” octomap_pa_msgs/GetSize Returning number nodes, total size bytes number inserted measurments. ”~/save” octomap_pa_msgs/FileName Storing current octomap file timestamps saved. ”~/load” octomap_pa_msgs/FileName Loading octomap file timestamps Parameters degrading voxels Parameter Type Description “~/degrading_time” double Duration long outdated nodes “~/auto_degrading” bool Turns automatic degrading. “~/auto_degrading_intervall” double Intervall automatic degrading. pointcloud insertion Parameter Type Description “~/map_prob_hit” double Probability positive measurement relates occupied voxel. “~/map_prob_miss” double Probability negative measurement relates occupied voxel. “~/pcd_voxel_active” bool voxel-filter pointcloud insertion. “~/pcd_voxel_explicit” bool pcl-filter octomap-filter. “~/pcd_voxel_explicit_relative_resolution” double Relative resolution pcl-filter. octomap general Parameter Type Description “~/output_frame” string Coordinate system insertion output. “~/map_resolution” double Side length voxel meters). “~/map_prob_threshold” double Threshold binary evaluation single voxels. “~/map_clamp_min” double clamping occupancy probability. “~/map_clamp_max” double Upper clamping occupancy probability. topics services Parameter Type Description “~/topic_in_cloud” string input topic pointclouds. “~/topic_in_cloud_old” string input topic pointclouds. “~/topic_in_laser” string input topic laser scans. “~/topic_out_octomap” string output topic binary octomap. “~/topic_out_octomap_full” string output topic full octomap. “~/topic_out_cload_free” string output topic free voxels. “~/topic_out_cloud_occupied” string output topic occupied voxels. config file. parameters default Links Source code github https://github.com/TUC-ProAut/ros_octomap
