omronsentech_camera package contains a basic functionality to access OMRON SENTECH Camera. This package requires installation of SentechSDK.
readme: ROS上のOMRON SENTECHカメラSDK omronsentech_cameraパッケージはオムロン 本パッケージ対応カメラはLinux版のSentechSDK対応カメラと同様です。 カメラの機能やGenICam GenApiノードのアクセスはSentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) GenICam GenApiノードとROSにあるノードとは全く別の物であることを注意下さい。 各GenICam GenApiノードは、汎用インタフェースを介してアクセス可能なカメラデバイスの機能またはレジスタに対応しています。GenICam GenApiの詳細はGenICam GenApiドキュメント（英語）を参照下さい。 本パッケージはUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-KineticとUbuntu 64ビット上のROS-Melodicで動作確認済みです。 SentechSDKのカメラ制御機能(Transport Layer) モジュールを利用するため、本パッケージをインストールする際は事前にSentechSDKをインストールする必要があります。下記のURLでSentechSDK (USB3VisionとGigEVision)がダウンロードできます： https://sentech.co.jp/products/USB/software.html. 本パッケージは下記のROSパッケージが必要となります： camera_info_manager image_transport message_generation sensor_msgs std_msgs pluginlib nodelet roscpp roslaunch roslint rospy インストール手順として、catkinのワークスペースにomronsentech_camera.gitをクローンするかこのレポジトリに全ファイルをコピーして下さい。 通常のROSパッケージとしてパッケージをビルドする前にSentechSDKの環境変数の設定を確認して下さい: echo $STAPI_ROOT_PATH 環境変数が未設定の場合は、下記のコマンドを実行して下さい（SentechSDKを/opt/sentechにインストールした場合）： source /opt/sentech/.stprofile omronsentech_cameraの内部構成は以下のとおりです。 |-./README.md |-./README-ja.md |-./CMakeLists.txt |-./nodelet_plugins.xml |-./package.xml |-./rosdoc.yaml |-./doc/ |-./html/ |-./mainpage-en.md |-./mainpage-ja.md |-./manifest.yaml |-./examples/ |-./cpp/grabber.cpp |-./python/grabber.py |-./include/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.h |-./launch/ |-./*.launch |-./*.yaml |-./msg/ |-./*.msg |-./src/ |-./omronsentech_camera/*.cpp |-./srv/ |-./*.srv docフォルダにあるドキュメントを生成する場合、omronsentech_cameraフォルダで下記のコマンドを実行下さい： rosdoc_lite doc 注意：Doxygenとrosdoc_liteパッケージが必要となります。 stcamera_nodeノード パッケージにあるstcamera_nodeはパラメータcamera_to_connectにより自動的に最初見つけたカメラ、指定したカメラや全カメラと接続し、画像を取得します。 stcamera_nodeノードの実行は次のコマンドのいずれかを実行下さい： rosrun omronsentech_camera stcamera_node roslaunch omronsentech_camera stcamera_node.launch ノードをstcamera_node.launchファイルスクリプトで起動するとカメラ接続パラメータがstcamera_node.yamlで簡単に設定できます。 camera_to_connect 接続可能なカメラの設定。このパラメーターは、stcamera_nodeが実行される前に設定する必要があります。 実行中にこのパラメーターを変更しても、その変更は反映されません。設定無しの場合、最初に見つけたカメラを接続します。”all”を設定する場合、見つけた全てのカメラを接続します。CAMERA_ID又はCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)を設定する場合、指定したカメラのみと接続します。例： camera_to_connect:[]: camera_to_connect:["all"]: camera_to_connect:["00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx","STC-MCS510U3V(00XXYY0)"]: MACアドレス「00:11:1c:f6:yy:xx」のGigEVisionカメラとシリアル番号「00XXYY0」のUSB3Visionカメラ(STC-MCS510U3V)のみ接続します。 camera_to_connect:["14210003XXYY"]: ID「14210003XXYY」のカメラのみ接続します. default_calibration_file 指定するキャリブレーションファイルがないカメラを利用するデフォールトキャリブレーションファイルパス。stcamera_nodeが実行中にこのパラメータが変更され、カメラもすでに接続されている場合は、変更を適用するためにカメラの画像取得を無効にして再度有効にして下さい。キャリブレーションファイルの作成と使い方は下記のROSのチュートリアルを参照下さい（英語）： http://wiki.ros.org/camera_calibration/Tutorials/MonocularCalibration http://wiki.ros.org/camera_calibration_parsers http://wiki.ros.org/image_proc {dev_CAMERA-NS}/calibration_file ネームスペース「{dev_CAMERA-NS}」が持っているカメラ用のキャリブレーションファイルパス。stcamera_nodeが実行中にこのパラメータが変更され、カメラもすでに接続されている場合は、変更を適用するためにカメラの画像取得を無効にして再度有効にして下さい。カメラネームスペースの詳細は次の章をご参照下さい。 stcamera_nodeはカメラと接続する際にカメラに対するトピックとサービスを宣言します。接続されたカメラを区別するために、各カメラには独自の名前空間（ネームスペース）があります。カメラのネームスペースは下記のルールで自動的に生成します： camera_to_connectの設定無又は[“all”]の場合、ネームスペースの形式は dev_{CAMERA_ID}になります。{CAMERA_ID} はカメラのID。接続されたカメラIDが「14210003xxYY」の場合、ネームスペースは「dev_14210003xxYY」になります. GigEVisionのMACアドレスなどのカメラIDの英数字以外の文字は、アンダースコアに置き換えられます。 camera_to_connectがCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)或いはCAMERA_IDの場合、ネームスペースの形式はdev_{CAMERA_MODEL_SERIAL_}或いは dev_{CAMERA_ID}になります。{CAMERA_MODEL_SERIAL_}はCAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)からの書き換えられた文字列です. {CAMERA_ID} {dev_CAMERA-NS}の値はサービスコールget_device_listまたdevice_connectionトピックでも得られます。 stcamera_nodeが発行するトピックは下記の通りです： device_connection {dev_CAMERA-NS}/camera_info ROSのimage_transport基準です。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/chunk {dev_CAMERA-NS}/event {dev_CAMERA-NS}/image_raw* 画像データを取得したときに発行されます（ROSのimage_transport基準）。 stcamera_nodeが提供するサービスは下記の通りです： （下記に記載するGenICamノードはROSのノードとは全く別の物です） get_device_list get_gige_ip 指定したGigEVisionカメラのIPアドレスを取得します。 get_module_list 対応するGenICam GenTLモジュール名のリストを取得します（System、Interface、LocalDevice、RemoteDevice、DataStream）。 モジュール名は特定のGenICamノードにアクセスする際に引数として渡す必要があります。たとえば、readまたはwrite_nodeサービス経由でカメラのGainにアクセスするには、GainがRemoteDeviceモジュールに存在するように指定する必要があります。 get_sdk_info SentechSDKバージョンとGenTLプロデューサの情報を取得します。 set_gige_ip 指定したGigEVisionカメラのIPアドレスを設定します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_chunk {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_event_acquisition {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_event_node 指定されるGenICamノードのイベントコールバックを有効または無効にします。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_image_acquisition {dev_CAMERA-NS}/enable_trigger {dev_CAMERA-NS}/execute_node 指定されるCommandインタフェースタイプを持つ特定のGenicamノードを実行します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_chunk_list {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_enum_list 指定されるGenICam列挙ノードの列挙のリストを取得します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_event_acquisition_status_list {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_event_node_status_list 指定されるモジュールのGenICamノードイベント取得のステータスを取得します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_genicam_node_info 指定されるGenICamノードの情報を取得します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_image_acquisition_status {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_last_error {dev_CAMERA-NS}/get_trigger_list {dev_CAMERA-NS}/image_raw* ROSのimage_transportに基づくサービスです. {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node ノードインタフェースタイプに関係なく、指定されるGenICamノードを読み込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_bool ブール型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_enum 列挙型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_float Floatのインターフェイスタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_int 整数のインターフェイスタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/read_node_string Stringのインターフェイスタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを読み取ります。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/send_soft_trigger {dev_CAMERA-NS}/set_camera_info ROSのimage_transportに基づくサービスです. {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node ノードインタフェースタイプに関係なく、指定されるGenICamノードを書き込みます。stcamera_nodeの内部は自動的に渡される文字列値をGenICamノードのインタフェースタイプに変換します。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_bool ブール型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードを書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_enum_int 列挙型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_enum_str 列挙型のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに文字列値（列挙型エントリの記号名）を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_float Floatのインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_int 整数のインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 {dev_CAMERA-NS}/write_node_string Stringのインタフェースタイプを持つ指定されるGenICamノードに整数値を書き込みます。 機種によって画像取得中にはGenICamノードにアクセスできない場合があります（エラー発生）。その際には画像取得を停止にしてから再度設定やアクセスして下さい。サービスコールenable_image_acquisitionで画像取得停止ができます。例： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_CAMERA-NS/enable_image_acquisition false IntegerタイプのGenICamノードは設定できる倍数が決まっており、その倍数で設定しないとエラーになります。サービスコール get_genicam_node_infoで倍数の確認ができます。下記の例の倍数は16 (increment: rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_CAMERA-NS/get_genicam_node_info RemoteDevice Width "Width" description: "Width image device pixels)." name_space: "Standard" interface_type: "IInteger" access_mode: "Read is_cachable: visibility: "Beginner" caching_mode: "Write register, write cache read" is_streamable: True enum_value_str_list: enum_value_int_list: current_value: min_value: max_value: increment: unit: child_node_list: GenTLエラーコード。GenICam GenTLドキュメント（英語）をご参照下さい。 GenICamのジェネリック例外が発生しました。 (GenICam) カメラのGainを変更し、image_viewで取得画像を表示する 全カメラとの接続を設定し、stcamera_nodeを実行します: roslaunch omronsentech_camera stcamera_node.launch rosservice call /stcamera_node/get_device_list device_list: timestamp: secs: nsecs: device_id: device_model: "STC-MCS510U3V" device_serial: "XXYYYZZ" device_tltype: device_tl_specific_field: device_tl_specific_value: device_gentl_info: vendor: "OMRON_SENTECH" version: producer_version: "1.5.X.X.X" full_path: "/opt/sentech/lib/libstgentl.cti" tltype: "Mixed" device_namespace: "dev_14210XXYYYZZ" connected: True image_viewを実行します： rosrun image_view image_view image:=/stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/image_raw、dev_14210XXYYYZZは接続されているカメラのネームスペースです。 カメラのGain（IFloatタイプ）を100.0に変更する場合は、次のようにGenicam書き込みノード・サービスを使用します： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/write_node_float RemoteDevice Gain rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/write_node RemoteDevice Gain 現在のGain値を読み取るには、次のようにGenICam読み取りノードサービスを使用します： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/read_node_float RemoteDevice rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/read_node RemoteDevice Gain get_genicam_node_info でGainノード情報を確認する場合は： rosservice call /stcamera_node/dev_14210XXYYYZZ/get_genicam_node_info RemoteDevice Gain "Gain" description: "Controls selected gain absolute physical amplification\ factor applied video signal." name_space: "Standard" interface_type: "IFloat" access_mode: "Read Write" is_cachable: visibility: "Beginner" caching_mode: "Write register, write cache read" is_streamable: True enum_value_str_list: enum_value_int_list: current_value: min_value: max_value: increment: unit: child_node_list: Launchファイルスクリプトの事例 Launchファイルスクリプトの例とそのパラメータはパッケージに含まれています。 stcamera_node.launch: このLaunchファイルスクリプトはstcamera_nodeを実行します。検出されたカメラと接続します。 このスクリプトはstcamera_node.yamlにキャリブレーションファイル（オプション）を設定せず、そのまま起動することができます。 stcamera_nodelet_manager.launch: このLaunchファイルスクリプトは、nodeletバージョンのstcamera_nodeを実行します。検出されたカメラと接続します。このスクリプトはstcamera_nodelet_manager.yamlにキャリブレーションファイル（オプション）を設定せず、そのまま起動することができます。 stcamera_node_imageproc.launch: このLaunchファイルスクリプトは、接続されたカメラからストリーミングされた画像を表示するstcamera_nodeとimage_viewという2つのノードを実行します。 このスクリプトを起動する際に、stcamera_node_imageproc.launchとstcamera_node_imageproc.yamlにCAMERA_IDまたはCAMERA_MODEL（SERIAL）および名前空間dev_CAMERA-NSの指定が必要となります： stcamera_node_imageproc.launchにあるcamera_namespace： <arg name="camera_namespace" default="dev_CAMERA-NS" stcamera_node_imageproc.yamlにあるcamera_to_connect： camera_to_connect: ["CAMERA_ID CAMERA_MODEL(SERIAL)"] stcamera_node_imageproc.yamlにあるdefault_calibration_file又は{dev_CAMERA-NS}/calibration_file：default_calibration_file: file:///home/mydata/calibration.xml ROSノードクライアント事例 exampleフォルダにあるノード「grabber」はシンプルなROSノードクライアント（両方C++とPythonベースがあります）事例として提供します。Grabberは下記の処理を行います： サービス「get_sdk_info」を呼び出して、SentechSDKとGenTL情報を取得し、画面に表示します。 サービス「get_device_list」を呼び出して、接続済みのカメラを検出し、カメラのネームスペースを取得します。 サービス「get_genicam_node_info」と「write_node_float」を呼び出して、カメラのGain値を読み取りと書き込みます。 トピック「image_raw」を参照して、十枚まで画像を取得し、画像サイズとエンコード情報を画面に表示します。 サービス「enable_trigger」を呼び出して、トリガモードを有効にして、十回まで一秒間ごとにサービス「send_soft_trigger」を呼び出して、画像を取得します。トリガモードを有効にする際、セレクタはFrameStartを指定し、ソースはSoftwareを指定します。 GigEVisionカメラを利用する場合は、カメラに有効なIPアドレスがあることを確認して下さい。IPアドレスは、サービス「get_device_list」または「get_gige_ip」を呼び出すことで確認できます。静的IPアドレスは、サービス「set_gige_ip」で設定できます。例： rosservice call /stcamera_node/get_gige_ip "00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx" （MACアドレス「00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx」を持っているGigEVisionカメラのIPアドレスを取得する場合）、及び rosservice call /stcamera_node/set_gige_ip "00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx" （MACアドレス「00:11:1c:xx:xx:xx」を持っているGigEVisionカメラの静的IPアドレスを設定する場合） stcamera_nodeは、OMRON SENTECHカメラを制御するために、一般的な機能を提供します。特定のカメラに対応するためのカスタマイズが必要な場合は、StCameraInterfaceクラスを継承し、別途のROSノードを作成するか既存のstcamera_node（StCameraNodeクラス）を編集する必要があります。stcamera_node（StCameraNodeクラス）を編集する場合、StCameraNodeクラスのinitializeCamera()関数にあるカメラインターフェイス初期化プロセスを編集する必要があります。 StCameraInterfaceやStCameraNodeクラスの構造については、doxygenドキュメントを参照下さい。 API コードのAPIやMsg/Srv APIのドキュメントは下記のURLでアクセスできます：https://wiki.ros.org/omronsentech_camera.

description: ROS driver to interface with ALB device.
readme: outsight_alb_driver ROS1 package Outsight Augmented Lidar Box (ALB). Author: Outsight Installation Prerequisite ROS (Install ROS) Functionnal ALB Install apt dependencies driver apt-get update ROS Noetic release xargs apt-get install <apt-dependencies_noetic.txt ROS Melodic release xargs apt-get install <apt-dependencies_melodic.txt Building sources Clone repository Build ROS catkin build system catkin_ws/src git clone https://gitlab.com/outsight-public/outsight-drivers/outsight_alb_driver.git catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Configuration Configure ALB application Documentation. ip_address ip_port defined launch file launch/outsight_alb.launch able publish messages topics frames, set config/alb_tcp.yaml file. manage frames, frame names defined configuration file: fixed_frame_id defined default alb_fixed_frame represents frame processing starts. fixed reference frame. sensor_frame_id represents sensor frame (linked sensor), default alb_sensor_frame. base_frame_id represents robot base frame frame optional, transformation section information) map_frame_id fixed frame world link odometry origin map frame optional). Usage Run nodes defined, alb_data alb_services. roslaunch outsight_alb_driver outsight_alb.launch manage multiple configurations, config file dedicated /config folder) precised launched. roslaunch outsight_alb_driver outsight_alb.launch config_file:=custom_config.yaml Recommendation: ALB processing manually resumed, restart ROS processing preferable reset ROS node order account processed record modified. risk incorrect poses odometry. Nodes alb_data node handles received data ALB publishes ROS. Published topics /alb/point_cloud sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 /alb/pose geometry_msgs/PoseStamped /alb/egomotion geometry_msgs/PoseStamped /alb/odometry nav_msgs/Odometry /alb/time_reference sensor_msgs/TimeReference /alb/tracked_objects outsight_alb_driver/TrackedObjects /alb/augmented_cloud outsight_alb_driver/AugmentedCloud /alb/zones outsight_alb_driver/Zones Timestamps ALB ROS timestamps stamp previous topic messages. topic /alb/time_reference links ALB timestamp ROS timestamp. ALB timestamp, set /use_alb_time parameter ‘true’ config file. Profided transformation transformation robot base frame sensor frame instance URDF file), package broadcast transform fixed frame (defined initial pose robot) robot base frame. transformation broadcasted simply transformation fixed frame sensor frame. broadcaster disabled setting broadcast_tf parameter ‘false’ config file. static transform map frame fixed frame published. transform case relocalization reference map. transform link origin map position processing starts. parameter sets frame Zones message. relocalization disabled, map_frame fixed_frame default settings, transform tree structured map_frame fixed_frame base_frame sensor_frame Frames pose odometry Pose, coordinate frame map frame, case Relocalization, fixed frame, initial Pose LiDAR, case Ego-Motion contrast, odometry message, displacement frame current frame, fixed frame. alb_services node defines services interact ALB. Custom services files outsight_alb_driver/AlbConfig Service manage processing configuration ALB. putting configuration from/to ALB. outsight_alb_driver/AlbFile Service storage ALB, download upload files. Services information ALB API, refer API documentation /alb/processing/restart std_srvs/Trigger Service restart processing ALB. Trigger service require arguments, call command: rosservice call /alb/processing/restart /alb/processing/stop std_srvs/Trigger Service stop processing ALB. rosservice call /alb/processing/stop /alb/processing/get_config outsight_alb_driver/AlbConfig Service processing configuration ALB. argument empty string, configuration output roslaunch console. argument full filepath, configuration exported file. WARNING: Filepath absolute. rosservice call /alb/processing/get_config rosservice call /alb/processing/get_config "/home/username/Documents/out_config.json" /alb/processing/put_config outsight_alb_driver/AlbConfig Service put processing configuration ALB. formatted JSON format. rosservice call /alb/processing/put_config "/home/username/Documents/config_to_put.json" alb/storage/download outsight_alb_driver/AlbFile Service download file ALB. Call download map named map_name.ply ALB output file. rosservice call /alb/storage/download maps map_name.ply "/home/username/Documents/output_map.ply" command full argument description. rosservice call /alb/storage/download "category: 'maps' alb_filename: 'map_name.ply' filepath: '/home/username/Documents/output_map.ply'" alb/storage/upload outsight_alb_driver/AlbFile Service upload file ALB. description: Ethernet interface to OxTS GPS receivers (NCOM packet structure)
readme: Ethernet interface OxTS GPS receivers NCOM packet structure Tested RT3000v2 receiver. Published Topics gps/fix (sensor_msgs/NavSatFix) GPS position geodetic coordinates (latitude longitude) gps/vel (geometry_msgs/TwistWithCovarianceStamped) GPS velocity ENU frame linear.x Velocity due East linear.y Velocity due North linear.z Velocity gps/odom (nav_msgs/Odometry) GPS position heading UTM frame, velocity vehicle body frame pose.position.x UTM Easting meters pose.position.y UTM Northing meters pose.orientation Yaw quaternion representing heading UTM. mind UTM heading relative UTM grid, necessarily aligned due East. phenomenon “grid convergence”. Wikipedia details. twist.linear.x Local frame velocity vehicle twist.linear.y Local frame lateral velocity vehicle twist.angular.z Yaw rate vehicle rad/s imu/data (sensor_msgs/Imu) Orientation, angular rates, linear accelerations reported OxTS unit gps/pos_type (std_msgs/String) Position solution type reported OxTS unit (point position, differential pseudorange, RTK float, RTK integer, gps/nav_status (std_msgs/String) Navigation status reported OxTS unit. fix, vel, odom, imu/data topics published topic string “READY”. gps/time_ref (sensor_msgs/TimeReference) UTC equivalent GPS timestamp reported OxTS unit. Parameters interface Restrict single network interface, eth0. Default <empty> ip_address Local listen address. Default <empty> port Local UDP listen port. Default frame_id_gps frame-ID geodetic GPS position IMU. Default gps frame_id_vel frame-ID ENU GPS velocity. Default enu frame_id_odom frame-ID UTM odometry. Default utm usage rosrun oxford_gps_eth gps_node roslaunch oxford_gps_eth gps.launch FAQ Connected Oxford GPS <ip_address>:3000 GPS position velocity messages published. Data published OxTS unit reports completely initialized. Drive faster short period time powering OxTS unit initialize sensor. gps/nav_status string topic status initialization process. string shows “READY”, GPS position velocity messages start publishing.

readme: PACMod2 (Platform Actuation Control MODule) ROS Driver ROS node designed user control vehicle Supported Vehicles PACMod drive-by-wire system, board revision Note driver meant PACMod systems work newer PACMod systems. pacmod3 repo ROS driver PACMod systems. access DBC file defines interface PACMod pacmod1_2_dbc repo. Installation Install pacmod debian repository: sudo apt install apt-transport-https sudo 'echo "deb [trusted=yes] https://s3.amazonaws.com/autonomoustuff-repo/ $(lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/autonomoustuff-public.list' sudo apt update sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-pacmod2 Note: Previously pacmod driver released ROS buildfarm. changed Ubuntu (ROS2 Foxy ROS1 Noetic) package versions download, users greater control installed software downgrades upgrade breaks software dependencies. ROS API Launch Arguments pacmod_vehicle_type: set match vehicle use_kvaser: Set true Kvaser device Kvaser canlib drivers connect PACMod. description: ROS Package for controlling the AStuff PACMod with a Joystick
readme: PACMod Game Controller ROS package software controlling PACMod system game controller. pacmod_game_control ROS node subscribes gamepad input data coming ROS joy node interfaces pacmod3 ROS driver control PACMod. Installation Note: Previously pacmod_game_control released ROS buildfarm. changed Ubuntu (ROS2 Foxy ROS1 Noetic) package versions download, users greater control installed software downgrades upgrade breaks software dependencies. ROS1 Noetic ROS2: Install pacmod_game_control debian repository: sudo apt install apt-transport-https sudo 'echo "deb [trusted=yes] https://s3.amazonaws.com/autonomoustuff-repo/ $(lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/autonomoustuff-public.list' sudo apt update sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-pacmod-game-control ROS1 Melodic older sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-pacmod-game-control Controls Supported Controllers supported controllers Logitech FORT Robotics Safe Remote Control Logitech Steering Wheel Pedals XBOX Wired Wireless Controller NOTE: Logitech controller switch controller switching Ensure switch set addition, Logitech MODE button toggles green light, ensure green light event add support controller: Update controllers.h add class controller button axes mapping. Override functions default control scheme. Logitech Control Scheme control scheme Logitech control scheme similar controllers. Note: MODE button. MODE light times button mapping change. Action Button Notes Enable/Disable Center region Enable START Buttons pressed simultaneously enable by-wire mode Disable button pressed disable by-wire mode. Gear Selection Button Pad (right-hand side) Drive Reverse Neutral Park Steering Joystick Steering Left Joystick Brake Left Trigger Throttle Trigger Windsheild Wipers Left Bumper supported vehicle platforms Horn Bumper supported vehicle platforms Headlights Turn Signals Directional Pad (left-hand side) Left turn signal Left turn signal Headlights supported vehicle platforms Hazards supported vehicle platforms ROS API Launch Arguments launch_driver: Set true launch pacmod3 driver pacmod_game_control node. Defaults false. pacmod_vehicle_type: set vehicle type. launch file options. controller_type: set controller type. launch file options. steering_max_speed: maximum rotational speed steering wheel (rad/s). max_veh_speed: Maximum vehicle speed speed-based steering damping. accel_scale_val: Scale applied outgoing accel commands, decreasing sensitivity. Defaults brake_scale_val: Scale applied outgoing brake commands, decreasing sensitivity. Defaults use_socketcan: Set true socketCAN connect PACMod. set false assumed Kvaser device Kvaser canlib drivers connect PACMod. Defaults false. pacmod_can_hardware_id: hardware kvaser device, applies use_socketcan false. pacmod_can_circuit_id: circuit/channel PACMod plugged kvaser device, applies use_socketcan false. pacmod_socketcan_device: device SocketCAN channel PACMod plugged applies use_socketcan true.

description:
Trajectory controllers (joint-based and Cartesian) that forward trajectories
    directly to a robot controller and let it handle trajectory interpolation and execution.
readme: Pass-Through Controllers package ROS-controllers Interface mechanisms enable forwarding trajectories joint-based Cartesian) robot interpolation. Rationale idea controllers enable forwarding (pass incoming trajectories robot OEM driver-side care interpolating waypoints. application demands industrial scale trajectory execution, prefer interpolate ROS side. complete trajectory driver side beneficial smoothness execution avoid issues related sending (streaming) hoc commands, classically current ROS-control approaches. controllers implement simple action servers trajectory execution Cartesian joint-based). meant light-weight functionality deliberately limited starting trajectories, canceling providing real-time feedback progress. Note work hardware abstraction robot. robot specific hard generalize. similar read() write() functions ROS-control, implement robot-specific protocols. package interfaces implement feature. Controller .yaml config controller joint trajectories robot forward_joint_trajectories: type: "pass_through_controllers/JointTrajectoryController" joints: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 controller joint trajectories robot forward_cartesian_trajectories: type: "pass_through_controllers/CartesianTrajectoryController" joints: joint1 joint2 joint3 joint4 joint5 joint6 Examples integration tests package give impression controller. ur_robot_driver controllers. Adapting RobotHW pass_through_controllers expect JointTrajectoryInterface CartesianTrajectoryInterface, depending trajectory Registering RobotHW #include <pass_through_controllers/trajectory_interface.h> class YourRobot public hardware_interface::RobotHW Callbacks pass_through_controllers' events void startJointInterpolation(const hardware_interface::JointTrajectory& trajectory); void startCartesianInterpolation(const hardware_interface::CartesianTrajectory& trajectory); void cancelJointInterpolation(); void cancelCartesianInterpolation(); interfaces trajectory forwarding hardware_interface::JointTrajectoryInterface jnt_traj_interface_; hardware_interface::CartesianTrajectoryInterface cart_traj_interface_; implementation: YourRobot::YourRobot() Register callbacks trajectory passthrough jnt_traj_interface_.registerGoalCallback( std::bind(&HardwareInterface::startJointInterpolation, std::placeholders::_1)); jnt_traj_interface_.registerCancelCallback(std::bind(&HardwareInterface::cancelInterpolation, cart_traj_interface_.registerGoalCallback( std::bind(&HardwareInterface::startCartesianInterpolation, std::placeholders::_1)); cart_traj_interface_.registerCancelCallback(std::bind(&HardwareInterface::cancelInterpolation, void YourRobot::startJointInterpolation(const hardware_interface::JointTrajectory& trajectory) Robot-specific implementation void YourRobot::startCartesianInterpolation(const hardware_interface::CartesianTrajectory& trajectory) Robot-specific implementation void YourRobot::cancelJointInterpolation() Robot-specific implementation void YourRobot::cancelCartesianInterpolation() Robot-specific implementation execution finished robot, signal driver finished trajectory. vendor-specific code void trajectorySignalFromRobotReceived(robot_vendor::TrajectoryResult result) hardware_interface::ExecutionState final_state; switch (result) case robot_vendor::TrajectoryResult::TRAJECTORY_RESULT_SUCCESS: final_state hardware_interface::ExecutionState::SUCCESS; break; case urcl::control::TrajectoryResult::TRAJECTORY_RESULT_CANCELED: final_state hardware_interface::ExecutionState::PREEMPTED; break; case urcl::control::TrajectoryResult::TRAJECTORY_RESULT_FAILURE: final_state hardware_interface::ExecutionState::ABORTED; break; default: std::stringstream "Unknown trajectory result: urcl::toUnderlying(result); throw(std::invalid_argument(ss.str())); track controller running... interface send result (joint_forward_controller_running_) jnt_traj_interface_.setDone(final_state); (cartesian_forward_controller_running_) cart_traj_interface_.setDone(final_state); ROS_ERROR_STREAM("Received forwarded trajectory result forwarding controller running."); pass_through_controllers check interfaces call registered callbacks receiving action goals preemption requests. implementation robot-specific. order provide feedback pass_through_controllers’ action clients, periodically fill feedback buffers: (joint_forward_controller_running_) jnt_traj_interface_.setFeedback(feedback); (cartesian_forward_controller_running_) cart_traj_interface_.setFeedback(feedback); FAQ passthrough controllers trajectories robot interpolation. ROS side, possibilities interfere running action limited start preempt trajectory execution. robot side, additional ways interfere execution depend OEM robot program implementation respective driver. passthrough controllers support additional concept speed scaling, pause trajectories. examples explain passthrough controllers’ behavior Universal Robots ur_robot_driver. hit emergency stop? trajectory execution paused case internally setting speed scaling program resumes, trajectory execution continues. passthrough controllers wait execution finishes report execution failure due missing waypoints’ time requirements goal tolerance power-off system? aborts current trajectory execution. robot protective stop? handled similar hitting emergency stop. speed scaling set trajectory execution continues protective stop resolved. stop program? stops ROS-controllers aborts current trajectory execution. description:
This package provides a specialized joint_trajectory_controller that can be moved into holding state via service call.
  No further trajectories will be accepted/followed in this state.
readme: Overview package joint trajectory controller put holding mode service call. specialized version ros_controllers::JointTrajectoryController providing holding mode functionality. controlled stop hold trajectory performed stopping manipulator mechanical stress hard brake. Topic interface deprecated: Due safety reasons, command interface PilzJointTrajectoryController deactivated. information issue ros-controls/ros_controllers. Speed monitoring controller perform speed monitoring robot links cartesian space. robot links move faster mm/s, controlled stop executed. requirement operation mode DIN ISO prbt_hardware_support. speed monitoring activated default. Additionally controller limits joint acceleration performed trajectories. file manipulator_controller.yaml limits adjusted. ROS API Advertised service is_executing (std_srvs/Trigger) Detect controller executing trajectory monitor_cartesian_speed (std_srvs/SetBool) Activate/deactivate speed monitoring hold (std_srvs/Trigger) Switch holding mode unhold (std_srvs/Trigger) Leave holding mode

description:
draco_point_cloud_transport provides a plugin to point_cloud_transport for sending point clouds
    encoded with KD tree compression.
readme: DRACO POINT CLOUD TRANSPORT Plugin ROS package point_cloud_transport, Google Draco compression library low-bandwidth lossy transportation PointCloud2 messages. Publisher plugin dynamic reconfiguration parameters, change compression runtime. Draco encode invalid values, pointclouds invalid values turned unstructured dense clouds NaNs, height Encode Decode Speed adjusting encode_speed dedode_speed parameters, adjust speed plugin encodes point cloud. higher number, higher speed encoding/decoding, worse compression. detailed information, documentation Draco. Encode Method Auto method decides encoding method based parameter default method. KD-tree method forces encoder KD-tree encoding. attribute point cloud type float32, kd-tree encoding requires attribute quantized. Sequential method forces encoder sequential encoding. Quantization sequential encoding. Sequential encoding worse compression KD-tree, faster arrangement points point cloud intact. sequential encoding encode point clouds Kinect. Deduplicate Deduplicate option tells encoder delete duplicate points point cloud, allowing transport smaller point clouds. Force Quantization Force_quantization option forces quantization KD-tree encoding method. Quantization Attribute Types Quantization_POSITION, Quantization_NORMAL, Quantization_COLOR tells encoder bits quantization attribute type. Attribute type point cloud attribute recognized based list names: POSITION POSITION POSITION “pos” POSITION “position” POSITION “rgb” COLOR “rgba” COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR COLOR NORMAL NORMAL NORMAL encoded GENERIC custom quantization, edit list recognized names expert_quantization expert_attribute_type options. Expert Quantization Expert_quantization option encoder custom quantization values point cloud attributes. Multiple POSITION attribute encoded varying quantization levels. set quantization PointField entry point cloud advertised base topic base_topic, set parameter: /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x. rosparam set /base_topic/draco/attribute_mapping/quantization_bits/x expert_quantization, user quantization bits PointField entries point cloud. The pr2_gripper_action provides an action interface for using the
  gripper. Users can specify what position to move to (while limiting the
  force) and the action will report success when the position is reached or
  failure when the gripper cannot move any longer.

The PR2 head action is a node that provides an action interface for
  pointing the head of the PR2.  It passes trajectory goals to the
  controller, and reports success when they have finished executing.

The pr2_gripper_sensor_action package provides an action interface for talking to the pr2_gripper_sensor_controller real-time controller.

description: The pr2_gripper_sensor_controller package is a real-time controller that integrates signals from the PR2 hand-mounted accelerometer and finger-mounted pressure sensors with motor control of the gripper joint to do highly responsive sensing and low-latency closed-loop control.

description:
The pr2_gripper_sensor_msgs package contains various actions and messages that are used in the pr2_gripper_sensor* packages. The structure of the API used by pr2_gripper_sensor_action, and pr2_gripper_sensor_controller packages is as follows: 

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

description:
This package contains the C++ interfaces to the PR2 hardware
  components that are controlled over EtherCAT. This includes the
  motors and encoders needed to control the PR2 mechanism, as well as
  components like the pressure sensors in the fingertips, camera
  triggers, etc... All of the hardware components in this interface are
  directly available to the controllers inside the hard realtime
  control loop.
The pr2_mechanism stack contains the infrastructure to control the PR2 robot in a hard realtime control loop.
The `pr2_mechanism_diagnostics` node subscribes to `mechanism_statistics` and publishes diagnostics data for joints and controllers on `/diagnostics`.

This package provides a node that monitors the state of the run stops of the pr2_robot. When the state of the
   run stop changes from off to on, this node will automatically enable the power to the motors, and reset
   the motors. This allows you to use the run stop as a 'pause' button. By using the run stop as a tool to
   power up the robot, the run stop is also in reach of the user once the robot starts moving.

description: quaternion operations
readme: pyquaternion fully featured, pythonic library quaternion representation, manipulation, animation geometry. visit pyquaternion homepage full information latest documentation. Designed Python Smooth animation interpolation random orientations pyquaternion module. Quickstart Install PyPI pip install pyquaternion Run basic overview. copy [demo.py](./demo/demo.py). ```python import pyquaternion Create quaternion representing rotation degrees positive axis. my_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, degrees=90) my_vector my_rotated_vector my_quaternion.rotate(my_vector) print('\nBasic Rotation') print('--------------') print('My Vector: {}'.format(my_vector)) print('Performing rotation {angle} deg {axis}'.format(angle=my_quaternion.degrees, axis=my_quaternion.axis)) print('My Rotated Vector: {}'.format(my_rotated_vector)) Create quaternion representing rotation null_quaternion pyquaternion.Quaternion(axis=[0, angle=0) print('\nInterpolated Rotation') print('---------------------') create sequence intermediate quaternion rotation objects pyquaternion.Quaternion.intermediates(null_quaternion, my_quaternion, include_endpoints=True): my_interpolated_point q.rotate(my_vector) print('My Interpolated Point: {point}\t(after rotation {angle} deg {axis})'.format( point=my_interpolated_point, angle=round(q.degrees, axis=q.axis print('Done!') output: Basic Rotation Vector: Performing rotation deg Rotated Vector: Interpolated Rotation Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg Interpolated Point: rotation deg

description:
This stack provides Python bindings for Qt.
    There are two providers: pyside and pyqt.  PySide is released under
    the LGPL.  PyQt is released under the GPL.

    Both the bindings and tools to build bindings are included from each
    available provider.  For PySide, it is called "Shiboken".  For PyQt,
    this is called "SIP".

    Also provided is adapter code to make the user's Python code
    independent of which binding provider was actually used which makes
    it very easy to switch between these.
authors: Dave Hershberger, Dorian Scholz, Dirk Thomas

razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. It also provides Arduino firmware that runs on the board, and which must be installed on it for the system to work. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.
authors: Tang Tiong Yew, Kristof Robot, Paul Bouchier, Peter Bartz

description:
The rc_dynamics_api provides an API for easy handling of the dynamic-state data
      streams provided by Roboception's stereo camera with self-localization.
      See http://rc-visard.com

      Dynamic-state estimates of the rc_visard relate to its self-localization and
      ego-motion estimation. These states refer to rc_visard's current pose,
      velocity, or acceleration and are published on demand via several data streams.
      For a complete list and descriptions of these dynamics states and the
      respective data streams please refer to rc_visard's user manual.
authors: Heiko Hirschmueller, Christian Emmerich, Felix Endres

description:
GenICam/GigE Vision Convenience Layer.

      This package combines the Roboception convenience layer for images with the
      GenICam reference implementation and a GigE Vision transport layer. It is a
      self contained package that permits configuration and image streaming of
      GenICam / GigE Vision 2.0 compatible cameras like the Roboception rc_visard.

      This package also provides some tools that can be called from the command line
      for discovering cameras, changing their configuration and streaming images.
      Although the tools are meant to be useful when working in a shell or in a
      script, their main purpose is to serve as example on how to use the API for
      reading and setting parameters, streaming and synchronizing images.

      See LICENSE.md for licensing terms of the different parts.

description: The rc_genicam_driver provides data from a Roboception rc_visard 3D sensor or rc_cube on several ROS topics.
readme: rc_genicam_driver Nodelet/node providing ROS interface configure Roboception rc_visard rc_cube receive images. consult manuals details: https://doc.rc-visard.com https://doc.rc-cube.com Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-genicam-driver Source rc_genicam_driver depends rc_genicam_api dependencies installed rosdep. rosdep install --from-paths rc_genicam_driver --ignore-src rc_genicam_driver Building installing package typical ROS catkin workflow. alternative, cmake build-flow mkdir build build cmake -DCATKIN_BUILD_BINARY_PACKAGE="1" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="/opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO" -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="/opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO" -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release install Alternatively, final install, package sudo dpkg install ros-melodic-rc-genicam-driver_*.deb. GenICam GenTL Transport Layer rc_genicam_driver rc_genicam_api interfacing rc_visard rc_cube GenICam/GigE Vision requires transport layer called GenTL producer (shared library suffix .cti). convenience rc_genicam_api producers Baumer common architectures. path producer set GENICAM_GENTL64_PATH environment variable GENICAM_GENTL32_PATH bit systems). set, rc_genicam_driver fall searching Baumer producer rc_genicam_api installed. producer .cti error message [ERROR] transport layers path /opt/ros/melodic/lib/rc_genicam_api case install rc_genicam_api properly set environment variable running export: GENICAM_GENTL64_PATH=/path/to/rc_genicam_api/baumer/Ubuntu-14.04/x86_64 Configuration Parameters Parameters set ROS param server run-time. device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor rc_cube. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline, setting rosparam launch file https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors internal generated GenTL producer. MAC address change implementation transport layer. https://github.com/roboception/rc_genicam_api#device-id details. default parameter set works compatible device network. gev_access: gev_access mode, ‘control’ Configuration streaming possibility clients read GenICam parameters. default. ‘exclusive’ Exclusive access sensor. prevents clients read GenICam parameters. Dynamic-reconfigure Parameters parameters changed runtime dynamic reconfigure: ptp_enabled: Enable PTP slave (PrecisionTimeProtocol, IEEE1588) camera_fps: Frames published nodelet. Publishing frames slowed depending setting. Setting higher real framerate specific device effect. camera_exp_auto: parameter removed. camera_exp_control camera_exp_control: Expose control mode “Manual” setting exposure time gain camera_exp_value camera_gain_value, “Auto” auto exposure “HDR” high dynamic range mode. Default: Auto. camera_exp_auto_mode Auto-exposure mode “Normal”, “Out1High” “AdaptiveOut1”. Default: Normal. camera_exp_max: Maximum exposure time seconds exp_auto true. camera_exp_auto_average_max: auto exposure set exposure time gain factor average image intensity upper bound. parameter defines upper bound. reached saturation due reflections). camera_exp_auto_average_min: camera_exp_auto_average_max. parameter defines bound. average image intensity reduced reduce avoid saturation due reflections). camera_exp_value: Exposure time seconds exp_auto false. camera_gain_value: Gain factor decibel exp_auto false. camera_gamma: Gamma factor. Default: Auto exposure region: Definition region left image, region size image, full left image determine auto exposure. camera_exp_width: Width auto exposure region. image. camera_exp_height: Height auto exposure region. image. camera_exp_offset_x: column auto exposure region camera_exp_offset_y: row auto exposure region depth_acquisition_mode: SingleFrame Continuous. letter checked, giving sufficient. depth_quality: Quality “Medium”, “High” “Full”. letter checked, specification sufficient. quality setting effectively downscales image downscale factor Full downscale image, factor NOTE: mode requires ‘stereo_plus’ license rc_visard. High downscales factor Medium downscales factor downscales factor depth_static_scene: parameter set true scene camera static. effect quality High Full. active, input images accumulated averaged reduce noise. limits frame rate maximum timestamp disparity image image accumulation. depth_fill: Higher numbers fill gaps measurments potentielly higher errors. depth_seg: Maximum size isolated disparity regions invalidated, related full resolution. depth_smooth: Switching smoothing disparities NOTE: Smoothing requires ‘stereo_plus’ license rc_visard. depth_minconf: Minimal confidence. disparities confidence set invalid. depth_mindepth: Minimum depth meter. disparities depth set invalid. depth_maxdepth: Maximum depth meter. disparities higher depth set invalid. depth_maxdeptherr: Maximum depth error meter. disparities higher depth error set invalid. depth_exposure_adapt_timeout: Maximum time seconds wait triggering SingleFrame modes auto exposure finished adjustments. out1_mode: Mode digital GPIO values switching permanently High switching permanently ExposureActive switching exposure time image. ExposureAlternateActive switching exposure time image. changed rc_visard Control license. default out2_mode: Mode digital GPIO functionality out1_mode. default color sensors, dynamic-reconfigure parameters additionally camera_wb_auto: true, white balancing automatically. false, red blue green ratios chosen manually. camera_wb_ratio_red: Red green ratio color balancing camera_wb_auto false. camera_wb_ratio_blue: Blue green ratio color balancing camera_wb_auto false. description: The rc_hand_eye_calibration_client package
readme: ROS Hand-eye calibration client Roboception’s rc_visard package developed anymore. client rc_reason_clients_ros repository. node ROS service calls topics calibrate rc_visard robot (aka hand-eye calibration). pre-existing calibration /tf_static. default behavior request existing calibration rc_visard startup, broadcast (latched) /tf_static broadcast advertised ROS services calibrate get_calibration called. calibration routine consists steps: Setting calibration parameters, grid size mounting, dynamic reconfigure. user-defined number robot calibration poses repeat Move robot pose (calibration grid visible rc_visard’s view). Send robot pose rc_visard (set_pose) Trigger calibration tranformation calculated (calibrate). calibration transform calculated tested, saved rc_visard (save_calibration). detailed instructions calibration routine consult rc_visard manual: https://doc.rc-visard.com. Installation Debian/Ubuntu add ROS sources sudo apt-get install ros-${ROS_DISTRO}-rc-hand-eye-calibration-client Source package relies git submodules cpr library initialized building source. git submodule update --init --recursive Configuration Parameters device: device, Roboception rc_visard sensor. serial number, IMPORTANT: preceed colon passing commandline setting rosparam https://github.com/ros/ros_comm/issues/1339). neccessary string launch file. user defined (factory default rc_visard’s model), unique reachable sensors. host: device address hostname rc_visard calibrated. rc_visard_frame_id: frame rc_visard calibrating. Default: “camera” end_effector_frame_id: frame calibrated robot_mounted rc_visard. Default: “end_effector”. base_frame_id: frame calibrated statically (externally) mounted rc_visard (robot_mounted false). Default: “base_link” calibration_request_period: Decimal number, controls requesting calibration rc_visard. Default: positive: Interval seconds automated requests latest calibration. request startup (default). negative, calibration requested. case, results broadcast services calibrate get_calibration node called. calibration_publication_period: Decimal number, controls broadcasting calibration /tf_static. Default: positive: Interval seconds. negative calibration broadcast /tf_static changed manual periodic calibration request (default). Dynamic reconfigure parameters grid_width: width calibration pattern meters. grid_height: height calibration pattern meters. robot_mounted: camera mounted robot tcp_rotation_axis:, TCP rotation axis DOF robot calibration general robot) tcp_offset: Offset TCP DOF robot calibration Services services offered follow calibration routine: reset_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Deletes previously poses images. saved calibration result reloaded. service (re-)start hand-eye calibration scratch. set_pose [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibrationPose]: robot pose calibration pose hand-eye calibration routine. calibrate [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Calculates returns hand-eye calibration transformation robot poses configured set_pose service. Broadcasts result /tf_static. get_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Calibration]: Returns existing hand-eye calibration transformation. Broadcasts result /tf_static. save_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Persistently saves result hand-eye calibration rc_visard overwrites existing stored result retrieved time get_calibration service. remove_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/Trigger]: Removes stored hand-eye calibration rc_visard. call get_calibration service reports hand-eye calibration Periodic broadcasting stopped. set_calibration [rc_hand_eye_calibration_client/SetCalibration]: Sets hand-eye calibration transformation. calibration transformation expected format returned calibrate get_calibration calls. save_calibration called calibration transformation persistent. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_hand_eye_calibration_client rc_hand_eye_calibration_client_node _device:=:<serial_number>

Services services offered node: detect: Triggers single detection. start_continuous_detection: Starts continuous detection. stop_continuous_detection: Stops continuous detection. Launching command line parameters: rosrun rc_tagdetect_client rc_april_node _device:=:<serial_number> rosrun rc_tagdetect_client rc_qr_node _device:=:<serial_number>

description: This package contains tools for the discovery of Roboception devices via GigE Vision.
readme: Discovery roboception sensors package tools discovery Roboception devices rc_visard rc_cube) GigE Vision. rcdiscover: console application discovering Roboception devices rcdiscover-gui: graphical application discovering Roboception devices sending magic packets resetting parameters Installation: find prebuilt packages Linux Windows releases page. Alternatively follow compilation steps sections. Compiling Linux compilation rcdiscover cmake required. rcdiscover-gui additionally requires FLTK. install Debian/Ubuntu sudo apt-get install cmake libfltk1.3-dev Building rcdiscover Building steps: mkdir build build cmake build gui pass CMAKE option BUILD_RCDISCOVER_GUI: cmake -DBUILD_RCDISCOVER_GUI=ON binaries build/tools/. Installation Installation install Debian derivatives Ubuntu) Debian packages built cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="/usr" package installed sudo dpkg rcdiscover*.deb Discovering sensors subnets Linux distributions reverse path filtering turned restricts discoverability sensor subnet host. Check sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter sysctl net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter Reverse path filtering turned sudo sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter=0 sudo sysctl net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter=0 disable specific interface, sudo sysctl net.ipv4.conf.eth0.rp_filter=0 Note: settings persistent reboots! persist add file /etc/sysctl.d/ distributions. debian/50-rcdiscover-rpfilter.conf built Debian package package, automatically disable reverse path filtering package installation. description: The remote_rosbag_record package
readme: remote_rosbag_record ROS nodes start/stop rosbag-record remote trigger Usage Launch recorder nodes favourite nodes. respawn options record multiple times. <launch> <group ns="console"> <include file="console.machine"/> console nodes <node name="console-record" pkg="remote_rosbag_record" type="record" respawn="true"> <rosparam file="console_topics.yml"/> <param name="prefix" value="console"/> <param name="apped_date" value="true"/> </node> </group> <group ns="robot"> <include file="robot.machine"/> robot nodes <node name="robot-record" pkg="remote_rosbag_record" type="record" respawn="true"> <rosparam file="robot_topics.yml"/> <param name="prefix" value="robot"/> <param name="apped_date" value="true"/> </node> </group> </launch> xxx_topics.yml topics: /joint_states /imu Start rosbag-record timing method remote_rosbag_record/trigger node rosrun remote_rosbag_record trigger _regex:="/.*/start" method remote_rosbag_record/joy_listener node rosrun remote_rosbag_record joy_listener _start_regex:="/.*/start" _start_button:=10 method call trigger services teleoperation node #include<remote_rosbag_record/call.hpp> #include<boost/regex.hpp> (start_button_pressed) remote_rosbag_record::call(boost::regex("/.*/start")); Stop rosbag-record (similar previous step) Nodes record starts/stops rosbag-record coressponding service called Services start (std_srvs/Empty) stop (std_srvs/Empty) Parameters parameters optional function original rosbag-record. http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag/Commandline#record ~record_all (bool) ~regex (bool) ~quiet (bool) ~append_date (bool) ~verbose (bool) ~compression (string) “uncompressed”, ~prefix (string) (string) ~topics (string array) ~buffer_size (int) ~exclude_regex (string) ~node (string) trigger calls multiple start/stop services Parameters ~regex (string, required) regular expression matches services call ~verbose (bool, default: true) verbose console output calling services joy_listener subscribes joystick messages calls start/stop services button pressed Subscribed topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy) Parameters ~start_button (int, default: button trigger start services ~start_regex (string, default: “<node’s namespace>/start”) regular expression matches start services ~stop_button (int, default: button trigger stop services ~stop_regex (string, default: “<node’s namespace>/stop”) regular expression matches stop services ~verbose (bool, default: true) verbose console output calling services

description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

description: The robot_navigation package
This package allows to use the Robot One Framework with ROS. The supported features of the framework includes the simulator and connection with the physical robots
readme: Robot-One-ROS ROS package Robot

description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.

description: Collection of gazebo plugins
readme: roboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_plugins Collection small gazebo plugins MimicJointPlugin simple (Model) plugin Gazebo order add Gazebo mimic joint functionality exists URDF (ROS). Inspired code Goncalo Cabrita. XML Parameters joint (Required) string joint mimic-ed. mimicJoint (Required) string mimic joint. multiplier double multiplier parameter mimic joint. Defaults offset double offset parameter mimic joint. Defaults maxEffort double max effort mimic joint generate. Defaults effort limit sdf model. sensitiveness double sensitiveness mimic joint. Defaults basically threshold difference angles (joint’s mimic’s) applying “mimicness”. robotNamespace string namespace robot Defaults hasPID Determines joint PID order controlled PID position/effort controller. <hasPID/> means mimic joint controlled PID. Omit mimic joint controlled setAngle. Optionally, set pid namespace. DisableLinkPlugin simple (Model) plugin Gazebo disable link Gazebo’s physics engine. XML Parameters link (Required) string link disabled. valid sdf urdf) link. Hoping add plugins…. Usage Standard Gazebo plugin import xacro/urdf. libroboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_ prefix. import MimicJointPlugin: libroboticsgroup_upatras_gazebo_mimic_joint_plugin.so Notes issue I’ll add functionality/plugin.     ROS topics at a meta level. None of the programs in this package actually

description: ROS to InOrbit topic republisher
readme: InOrbit republisher ROS directory includes republisher mapping arbitrary ROS values InOrbit custom data key/value pairs application-specific observability. mapping ROS topics supported. republisher extended map actions, services parameters. Usage Create YAML config file mappings format: republishers: topic: "/fruits_per_cubic_m" msg_type: "fruit_msgs/Citrus" mappings: field: "num_oranges" mapping_type: "single_field" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "oranges" topic: "/hardware/status" msg_type: "hw_msgs/HardwareStatus" mappings: field: "status" mapping_options: fields: ["lidar", "motor", "battery"] filter: 'lambda (x.status mapping_type: "array_of_fields" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "hardware_error" topic: "/cmd_vel" msg_type: "geometry_msgs/Twist" mappings: field: "linear" mapping_type: "json_of_fields" mapping_options: fields: topic: "/inorbit/linear_vel_test" key: "linear_vel" static_publishers: fixed string" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "greeting" value_from: environment_variable: "PATH" topic: "/inorbit/custom_data/0" key: "env_path" launch republisher.py script passing config file config param. suggested organize creating config file launch file package, launch file <launch> <node name="inorbit_republisher" pkg="inorbit_republisher" type="republisher.py"> <param name="config" textfile="$(find inorbit_republisher)/config/example.yaml" </node> </launch> Mapping ROS topics republisher map ROS values single field 'fruit=apple') array fields 'fruits=[{fruit1: apple, fruit2: orange}, {fruit1: melon, fruit2: apple}]'). capture simple fields data array values. Single field: mapping options republishing single field, include set mapping_options mapping. include: filter: lambda expression control published based condition. you’d republish String values SPAMMY STRING, mapping_options: filter: 'lambda "SPAMMY STRING")' Array fields: mapping options republishing array fields, include set mapping_options mapping. include: fields: set fields you’d capture array element. array element elements you’d mapping_options: fields: fields republisher fields array element. filter: lambda expression pick array elements based condition. you’d republish array elements mapping_options: filter: 'lambda JSON fields: mapping options republishing fields nested structure, mapping type bundle single JSON message republishing fields interest separately. mapping_options type include: fields: set fields you’d capture nested message. message definition Twist: setting mapping_options: fields: output JSON object fields keys respective values message data: "linear_vel={\"y\": filter: lambda expression control JSON object published based condition. you’d republish JSON objects key mapping_options: filter: 'lambda linear_vel: (linear_vel["z"] Publishing fixed values publish fixed values facilitate fleet-wide observability. publish environment variables, package versions fixed values static_publishers array. included configuration config/example.yaml specific examples. values published latched delivered time subscriber connects republisher. Building running locally Find instructions building package running node code workspace catkin). Build ~/catkin_ws rosdep install --from-paths ~/catkin_ws/src --ignore-src --rosdistro=melodic catkin clean catkin build inorbit_republisher --verbose Run ~/catkin_ws/install/setup.zsh launch file 'launch' directory roslaunch launch/example.launch TODO Schema validation documentation Error handling Proper logging Latched topic support Mapping ROS parameters

description: Contains aligned allocation functions, as well as an STL-compatible AlignedAllocator class.

The lockfree package contains lock-free data structures for use in multithreaded programming.  These
     kinds of data structures are generally not as easy to use as single-threaded equivalents, and are not
     always faster.  If you don't know you need to use one, try another structure with a lock around it
     first.
The ros_realtime package

rosatomic provides the C++11-style atomic operations by pulling symbols from the proposed Boost.Atomic
     package into the ros namespace.  Once C++11-style atomics (std::atomic) are available from compilers, rosatomic will
     conditionally use those instead.

description: rosserial for Windows platforms.

     nodes connected to an xbee. All nodes communicate back

     http://code.google.com/p/python-xbee/

description: Additiional testing tools at node level

ROS Python package for throttling ROS topics programatically in Python. Sits on top of the 
        ros_comm topic_tools throttle utility.
readme: rosthrottle ROS Python package throttling ROS nodes programatically Python. Sits top ros_comm topic_tools throttle utility. rosthrottle package types throttlers: MessageThrottle Bandwidth Throttle. MessageThrottle throttle topic message frequency basis. rosthrottle import MessageThrottle intopic 'in' outtopic 'out_throttled' rate MessageThrottle(intopic, outtopic, rate) start throttler process pid bt.start() update throttler configuration bt.update(rate=5.0) kill throttler bt.stop() BandwidthThrottle throttle topic bandwidth basis. rosthrottle import BandwidthThrottle intopic 'in' outtopic 'out_throttled' bandwidth window BandwidthThrottle(intopic, outtopic, bandwidth, window) start throttler process pid bt.start() update throttler configuration bt.update(bandwidth=100) kill throttler bt.stop() src/rosthrottle/tests/ additional examples documentation.

Dependencies rosdep Install, initialize update rosdep. Compiling Create catkin workspace clone project: mkdir catkin_workspace/src catkin_workspace/src git clone https://gitlab.com/InstitutMaupertuis/rqt_bag_exporter.git Resolve ROS dependencies rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro $ROS_DISTRO Compile catkin_make GUI user application Source catkin workspace compiled package, launch: roslaunch rqt_bag_exporter gui.launch desktop file installed installing project: open software application application menu. :information_source: delete installed files workspace desktop launcher deleted. located $HOME/.local/share/applications; remove file manually. Multi bag exporter roslaunch rqt_bag_exporter multi_bag_exporter.launch dir:="/home/victor/bags" topic_name_include:="/topic_test" merged_csv:=false full documentation arguments launch file launch/multi_bag_exporter.launch

readme: English rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverは株式会社アールティが販売しているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュール用のROSパッケージです。 License: 3-Clause BSD License Source: https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git (branch: master) 現在、以下のROSのディストリビューションに対応しております。 Kinetic (kinetic-devel) Melodic (melodic-devel) Noetic (noetic-devel) Dashing (dashing-devel) Foxy (foxy-devel) industrial_ci source build amd64 binary arm64 binary main develop(master) ROS develop(ros2-devel) Xenial Kinetic(kinetic-devel) Bionic Melodic(melodic-devel) Focal Noetic(noetic-devel) Bionic Dashing(dashing-devel) Focal Foxy(foxy-devel) このパッケージは、USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールをROSから使用するためのドライバを提供するものです。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールは、センサとしてInvenSense社のMPU9250を使用しております。 このセンサの磁気センサの座標系はNED座標系(x-north, y-east, z-down)ですが、 モジュール内のマイコン(LPC1343)においてENU座標系(x-east, y-north, z-up)に変換され、 これはROSで使われる座標系のルールにも適合しています。詳しくは、REP-0103をご覧ください。 USB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはオープンハード・オープンソースのため、モジュール内のマイコンのファームウェアの変更が可能です。 このROSパッケージはデフォルトのファームウェアにのみ対応しております。ファームウェアを変更された場合、正常な動作ができなくなる恐れがございますので、ご了承ください。 ver2.0でのご利用について 2018年10月現在、販売されているUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールはver2.0となります。 このバージョンのデフォルトのファームウェアには、ASCII出力とBinary出力の２つのデータ出力形式があります。 センサ出荷時点ではASCII出力に設定されています。出力形式の切り替え方法は、以下のリポジトリにあるマニュアルをご参照ください。 https://github.com/rt-net/RT-USB-9AXIS-00 ROS 2のパッケージについてはros2-develのREADMEをご覧ください。 ROS Kineticの場合 sudo apt install ros-kinetic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver ROS Melodicの場合 sudo apt install ros-melodic-rt-usb-9axisimu-driver catkinワークスペースを~/catkin_wsとすると、以下のような手順になります。 ~/catkin_ws/src git clone $ROS_DISTRO-devel https://github.com/rt-net/rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.git ~/catkin_ws catkin build 2のようにインストールを行った場合、ドライバを起動しようとする端末(Terminal)で、 source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash roscore rosrun rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver roslaunch rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driver.launch [ERROR] Error opening sensor device, re-check devices. sudo chmod /dev/ttyACM0 rt_usb_9axisimu_driver rt_usb_9axisimu_driverはUSB出力9軸IMUセンサモジュールの出力を受信し、角速度と並進加速度・磁束密度をパブリッシュします。 /imu/data_raw(sensor_msgs/Imu) /imu/mag(sensor_msgs/MagneticField) /imu/temperature(std_msgs/Float64) ~frame_id (string, default: imu_link) IMUデータのヘッダーにセットされるフレーム名 ~port (string, default: /dev/ttyACM0) ~linear_acceleration_stddev (double, default: ~angular_velocity_stddev (double, default: 角速度の共分散行列の対角成分の平方根(rad/s) ~magnetic_field_stddev (double, default:

This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.

     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.

     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state

     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data

     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

description: The seed_smartactuator_sdk package
readme: seed_smartactuator_sdk ROS上のパッケージから本ライブラリをインクルードし、SEED-Driverへコマンドを送信するための一連サンプルを示す。 なお、PCからSEED-Driverへ直接指令を行う際は通信モジュール(CM4U/CMSU)が必要となる。 多軸ロボットへ指令する際は、aero3_command.hを用いる。詳細は seed_r7_ros_controllerを参考にされたい #include <ros/ros.h> #include "seed_smartactuator_sdk/seed3_command.h" int main(int argc, char **argv) ros::init(argc,argv,"cpp_sample_node"); ros::NodeHandle seed::controller::SeedCommand seed; seed.openPort("/dev/ttyACM0",115200); seed.openCom(); int std::array<int,3> motor_state; seed.runScript(id,1); seed.waitForScriptEnd(1); (ros::ok()) motor_state seed.getPosition(id); if(motor_state[0]) std::cout motor_state[2] std::endl; return Python #!/usr/bin/env python import rospy seed_smartactuator_sdk.seed3_command import SeedCommand main "__main__": rospy.init_node('python_sample_node') seed=SeedCommand() seed.open_port("/dev/ttyACM0",115200) seed.open_com() motor_state seed.run_script(id,1) seed.wait_for_script_end(1) rospy.is_shutdown(): motor_state seed.get_position(id) if(motor_state[0]): print motor_state[2]

Package for using SkyWay from ROS
readme: SkyWay ROS ROSからSkyWayを利用するためのパッケージです。 端末間でP2P接続を行うための規格としてWebRTCが標準化されています。 SkyWayはWebRTCプラットフォームであり、SkyWayを利用することで、ブラウザ・iOS・Androidなどの環境で相互にWebRTC接続を簡単に行うことができます。 WebRTCは映像と音声を双方向通信するためのMediaStreamと、データを転送するためのDataChannelが定義されています。 Skyway ROSを利用することで、MediaStreamとDataChannelをROSパッケージから利用することができます。 SkyWay ROSでは2つのサービスが提供されており、これらを利用し操作を行うことで、外部プログラム(Peer)に対してMediaStreamとDataConnection(over DataChannel)の確立を行います。 SkyWayControl SkyWayEvents SkyWay ROS内のMediaSocketに対してRTP/RTCPを送信することで、確立されたMedia Connectionを経由して、Peerに対して映像と音声を送ることができます。 また、データ転送用のROS Pluginをユーザ側で定義することができ、そのPluginに対してROS Topic, Service, Actionなどを用いてデータを送受信することで、 Peerとデータのやりとりを行うことができます。 操作方法とAPI仕様 まずSkyWay ROS内に、SkyWayサーバとやり取りするために　Peer Objectを生成します。以下のドキュメントを参照してください。 PeerObjectの生成 PeerObjectの状態確認 Peer Objectが生成できたら、MediaConnection, DataConnectionの接続処理及び待ち受けが行えるようになります。以下のページを参照してください。 MediaConnectionの確立 MediaConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionの確立 DataConnectionの待ち受け DataConnectionが確立できたら、Pluginを介して外部ROS Moduleとデータのやり取りを行えます。 Pluginの仕様についてはこちらのドキュメントを参照してください。 tipsも参照して下さい。 examplesディレクトリ及びskyway_for_ros_examplesリポジトリを参照して下さい。

description:
This package contains a ROS wrapper for OpenSlam's Gmapping. 
  The gmapping package provides laser-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), 
  as a ROS node called slam_gmapping. Using slam_gmapping, you can create a 2-D occupancy
  grid map (like a building floorplan) from laser and pose data collected by a mobile robot.
slam_gmapping contains a wrapper around gmapping which provides SLAM capabilities.

This package pulls in the Karto mapping library, and provides a ROS
     wrapper for using it.
maintainers: Michael Ferguson, Luc Bettaieb, Russell Toris

description: These are the core packages for the Shadow Robot hardware and simulation.
readme: Service Status Documentation Code style checks Unit tests Shadow Robot Core Packages core packages Shadow Robot hardware simulation.

description: The sr_gazebo_sim package

This is a ROS interface to the Shadow Robot's robotic hand. It
        contains both an interface to the real hand (communicating via
        a CAN interface) and a simulated version of the hand. It also
        contains an interface to Shadow Robot's muscle arm.
This package contains the actuator used in the hand model.

The sr_mechanism_controllers package contains different types of
        controllers for the etherCAT hand: position controllers,
        velocity controllers, force controllers, ...

sr_mechanism_model contains the transmissions used in the robot model. We needed specific
        transmission as we're using our own actuator. We also needed to take care of the joint 0s
        which combine the distal and middle phalanges.

An interface to the tactile sensors used in the Shadow Dextrous Hand. Also Contains a virtual set of
        sensors.
sr_utilities contains different useful header libraries (math libraries, etc...).

sr_external_dependencies package is a "dummy" package used to synchronize the includes for the protocol

description: The swarm_ros_bridge package
readme: swarm_ros_bridge Introduction lightweight middle interface ROS package based ZeroMQ. enables ROS messages transmission swarm robots socket communication. purpose package replace traditional running ROS multiple machines ROS1, drawbacks swarm robots situation. ROS robots communicating swarm_ros_bridge shown Compared ROS1 multi-robot wireless communication, benefits: Robust: base station ROS master launching Support robot launching random sequence connecting autonomously. Flexible: choose sending/receiving ROS topics transferring topic (names) ROS1 Easy ROS topics configuration file. Compared ROS2 DDS communication, benefits: Lightweight: small ROS bridge node subscribing sending remote ROS topics, connecting ROS nodes easy. Reliable: ZeroMQ socket communication based TCP protocol ROS2 based DDS, default protocol UDP (unreliable). DDS designed data exchange native processes wired communication remote wireless communication. ROS wiki page: https://wiki.ros.org/swarm_ros_bridge source code: https://github.com/shupx/swarm_ros_bridge.git csdn blog chinese): https://blog.csdn.net/benchuspx/article/details/128576723 Structure swarm_ros_bridge CMakeLists.txt config ros_topics.yaml Config file send/receive ROS topics include bridge_node.hpp Header file bridge_node.cpp ros_sub_pub.hpp Header file ROS message type. launch test.launch package.xml src bridge_node.cpp Reliable TCP bridge ros data transfer unstable network. send/receive ROS topics ../config/ros_topics.yaml zmq socket(PUB/SUB mode), reconnects autonomously supports pub-sub connection TCP protocol. Install Supported platforms/releases: Platform ROS Release Ubuntu Xenial ROS Kinetic Ubuntu Bionic ROS Melodic Ubuntu Focal ROS Noetic Install process: clone package mkdir swarm_ros_bridge_ws/src ros workspace swarm_ros_bridge_ws/src git clone https://gitee.com/shu-peixuan/swarm_ros_bridge.git 'git clone https://github.com/shupx/swarm_ros_bridge.git' install dependencies sudo apt install libzmqpp-dev 'rosdep install --from-path swarm_ros_bridge/' build catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Usage ROS topic information config/ros_topics.yaml. sending topic, port binds “tcp://*:port”. receiving topic, port remote source port connects “tcp://srcIP:srcPort”. max_freq limit sending frequency exceeds max_freq. Set max_freq large decrease sending frequency. Launch bridge_node: roslaunch swarm_ros_bridge test.launch local machine test Publish messages send_topics check remote recv_topics receiving messages. console print INFO time recv_topics receive messages. provide simple latency test demo machines. refer scripts/README.md Advanced ROS message types default supported ROS message types sensor_msgs/Imu geometry_msgs/Twist std_msgs/String. types: Modify macros MSG_TYPEx MSG_CLASSx include/ros_sub_pub.hpp, generate template functions ros message types. ros_sub_pub.hpp uncomment modify lines: #include <xxx_msgs/yy.h> #define MSG_TYPE4 "xxx_msgs/yy" #define MSG_CLASS4 xxx_msgs::yy support types modification. modify topic_subscriber(), topic_publisher() deserialize_publish() include/ros_sub_pub.hpp styles. Add dependent package find_package() CMakeLists.txt: CMakeLists.txt find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS roscpp std_msgs geometry_msgs sensor_msgs xxx_msgs Recompile: swarm_ros_bridge_ws/ catkin_make send_topics support send_topics. Modify lines include/ros_sub_pub.hpp ros_sub_pub.hpp define SUB_MAX max number subscriber callbacks template <typename void (*sub_callbacks[])(const sub_cb<T,0>, sub_cb<T,1>, //add recompile: swarm_ros_bridge_ws/ catkin_make Future Work Dynamic RPC, including dynamic node discovery, online topic change, ground station monitor. Support UDP protocol mass data transmission video streams. Support ROS service transmission ZeroMQ request-reply mode. Support ROS2 topic service transmission. Contributor Peixuan Shu (shupeixuan@qq.com), PhD, beihang university, China, package personally developed Peixuan Shu (PhD, beihang university, China) Jan. question/suggestion welcomed swarm_ros_bridge issue shupeixuan@qq.com. applications: pyugvswarm python package wrapper unmanned ground vehicle (UGV) swarm positioning communication control ROS1 nodes. https://pyugvswarm.readthedocs.io/en/latest/overview.html

The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
    to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
    The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
    the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
    separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.
readme: teb_local_planner ROS Package teb_local_planner package implements plugin base_local_planner navigation stack. underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes robot’s trajectory respect trajectory execution time, separation obstacles compliance kinodynamic constraints runtime. Refer http://wiki.ros.org/teb_local_planner information tutorials. description: Script to delay the launch of a roslaunch file
readme: timed_roslaunch script delay launch roslaunch file. Usage script delay launch roslaunch file. file executable (chmod timed_roslaunch.sh) Run command line rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh [number seconds delay] [rospkg] [roslaunch file] [arguments (optional)] roslaunch timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.launch time:=[number delay] pkg:=[rospkg] file:=[roslaunch file] value:=[arguments (optional)] rosrun timed_roslaunch timed_roslaunch.sh turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" Run roslaunch file <launch> <include file="$(find timed_roslaunch)/launch/timed_roslaunch.launch"> <arg name="time" <arg name="pkg" value="turtlebot_navigation" <arg name="file" value="amcl_demo.launch" <arg name="value" value="initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0" <arg name="node_name" value="timed_roslaunch" optional jBrgment </include> </launch> <launch> <node pkg="timed_roslaunch" type="timed_roslaunch.sh" args="2 turtlebot_navigation amcl_demo.launch initial_pose_x:=17.0 initial_pose_y:=17.0"  name="timed_roslaunch" output="screen" </launch> LICENSE software released BSD 3-Clause License, LICENSE.txt.

description:
A light-weight, Eigen-based
    C++ library for trajectory optimization for legged robots. 
    This library provides implementations for variables, costs and constraints
    that can be used to represent a legged locomotion problem. 
    The resulting Nonlinear Programming Problem (NLP) can then be solved with
    off-the-shelf solvers, e.g. Ipopt using the generic optimizer interface
A ROS dependent wrapper for

The ROS packages in this repository were created to provide an improved
    alternative Inverse Kinematics solver to the popular inverse Jacobian
    methods in KDL.  TRAC-IK handles joint-limited chains better than KDL
    without increasing solve time.
This package contains the source code for testing and comparing trac_ik
readme: package examples programs standalone TRAC-IK solver related code. exists ik_tests program compares KDL’s Pseudoinverse Jacobian solver TRAC-IK. pr2_arm.launch files runs test default robot’s 7-DOF arm chain. package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik

description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.
